title: 360º Reorient Sphere
description: Reorients a 360º photo or video in equirectangular format.

parameters.label: Orientation
parameters.description:
The change in orientation from the camera's original position.
Default: 0.000000,-0.000000,0.000000

parameters.label: Rotation
parameters.description:
The change in rotation from the camera's original position.
Default: 0.000000,0.000000,0.000000

title: 360º Viewer
description: Projects a 360º photo or video onto a flat surface, as if viewed from within the 360º sphere.

parameters.label: Orientation
parameters.description:
The difference in orientation from the original camera position.
Default: 0.000000,-0.000000,0.000000

parameters.label: Rotation
parameters.description:
The difference in rotation from the original camera position.
Default: 0, 0, 0

parameters.label: Distance
parameters.description:
The distance of the surface of the 360º sphere from the viewer. Increasing this also effectively increases the field of view.
Range: 0.2 to 2; Default: 1

parameters.label: Aspect Ratio
parameters.description:
The target aspect ratio for the output of the effect. Can be any of the following:

16:9
4:3
1:1
4:5
9:16

Default: 16:9

parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description:
A scale factor to apply to the layer after the 360º projection is calculated but before the final pixel data is rendered. This is useful if you want the final rendered image to be a different size from the original, but without missing pieces or loss of resolution.
Range: 0.1 to 2; Default: 1

title: Auto-Shake
description: Moves the layer randomly over time within specified parameters.

title: Bend
description: Bends the layer along a circular arc.

parameters.label: Origin
parameters.description:
Defines the fixed side of the layer. It is the side opposite this which will move when the layer bends.

Left
Right
Top
Bottom

Default: Bottom

parameters.label: Angle
parameters.description:
The new angle for the side of the layer opposite the Origin.
Range: -180º to 180º; Default: 0º

parameters.label: Start
parameters.description:
The position within the layer at which the bend starts. For example, if this is set to 33%, the third of the layer nearest the origin will remained fixed, and the remaining two thirds will bend.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 0%

parameters.label: Before Start
parameters.description:
Controls how the part of the layer before Start is handled.

Freeze:  The part of the layer before Start is fixed and does not bend.
Bend to Same Side:  The part of the layer before Start bends in the same direction, mirroring the bend after Start.
Bend to Opposite Side:  The part of the layer before Start bends in the opposite direction, creating an "S" curve.

Default: Freeze

parameters.label: Tune Head
parameters.description:
Tune Head limits the bend at the end of the layer opposite the origin.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 75%

parameters.label: Tune Tail
parameters.description:
Tune Head limits the bend at the origin.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 25%

title: Blink
description: Automatically adjusts the opacity of the layer, alternating between its normal value and zero over time.

parameters.label: Frequency
parameters.description:
The number of complete cycles per second, where each complete cycle consists of an visible and invisible portion. Higher values result in faster blinking.  cycles/second
Range: 0 to 16; Default: 2

title: Block Dissolve
description: Randomly removes grid-aligned rectangular pieces from the layer by making them transparent. Keyframe animation can be applied to remove progressively more and more rectangles over time.

parameters.label: Strength
parameters.description:
The ratio of squares to be made transparent. At 0, all squares are fully opaque, and at 1 all squares are transparent and the layer becomes invisible.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.75

parameters.label: Size
parameters.description:
The size of each square, in pixels.
Range: 1 to 500; Default: 10

parameters.label: Stretch
parameters.description:
X and Y scaling factors to be applied to stretch the grid of squares.
Default: 1, 1

parameters.label: Seed
parameters.description:
A seed used to generate the randomness used in this effect. Change this for different patterns of randomness. This controls the order in which blocks disappear as Strength increases.  Animate this for quick, jittery motion over time.
Range: 0 to 5; Default: 0

parameters.label: Fade
parameters.description:
Controls whether squares become transparent immediately (when fade is zero) or gradually (when fade is greater than zero). This value represents how much Strength has to change by for a single square to go from fully opaque to fully transparent.
Range: 0 to 0.5; Default: 0

parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description:
Adjusts the position of the grid of squares relative to the layer.
Default: 0, 0

title: Block Noise
description: Multiplies randomly generated rectangular blocks of noise with the existing colors of opaque parts of the layer.

parameters.label: Size
parameters.description:
The size of the blocks in the noise pattern.
Range: 1 to 999; Default: 20

parameters.label: Stretch
parameters.description:
Stretches or compresses the blocks in the noise pattern along the X or Y axis independently. Use this to adjust the aspect ratio of the blocks.
Default: 0, 0

parameters.label: Strength
parameters.description:
The overall strength of the effect. At 0, the effect does nothing, leaving the layer unchanged.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.25

parameters.label: Monochrome
parameters.description:
If this is turned on, the randomly generated blocks will be a uniform gray color and when multiplied with the colors in the layer will affect the layer brightness only. If turned off, the randomly generated blocks can be any color.
Default: off

parameters.label: Freeze
parameters.description:
Controls automatic variance over time. Turn this on to freeze the noise pattern, causing it to remain constant over time. If this this is off, the noise pattern will change randomly with every frame.
Default: off

parameters.label: Extremes
parameters.description:
Forces blocks in the noise pattern to appear either at full strength or not at all. In-between strengths are not generated.
Default: off

parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description:
Shifts the random block pattern horizontally or vertically by the given amount.
Default: 0, 0

parameters.label: Overshoot
parameters.description:
The amount by which RGB noise components can overshoot the maximum possible values. If this is set to 1.0 the generated components will be at most one, so when multilied with colors in the layer can only make the existing colors darker.  Setting this to a value greater than one yields random RGB values that can over over 1.0 and can thus make colors in the layer brighter. Note that when this is greater than 1.0, color clipping may occur.
Range: 0.5 to 2; Default: 2

parameters.label: Seed
parameters.description:
A value used to generate the random patterns of noise. Change this for different noise patterns. For control over when the noise pattern changes, turn on Freeze and animate Seed instead.
Range: 0 to 5; Default: 0

title: Box
description: Renders a faux-3D box using the layer as a surface texture.

parameters.label: Orientation
parameters.description:
The orientation of the 3D object. This is similar to Rotation, but is interpreted differently when animated using Keyframes. /Orientation animates along the nearest path between different poses, and is useful for posing an object or animating an object between poses. Orientation values can't go out of range, so you can't use Orientation to "spin" an object (for that, use Orientation without animation to pose the object, and animate Rotation to create the spin)
Default: 0, 0, 0

parameters.label: Rotation
parameters.description:
The rotation of the 3D object. This is similar to Orientation, but the X, Y and Z components are interpreted independently when animated and can go out of range. This makes Rotation very handy for animating spinning motion, but not as convenient for creating arbitrary poses or animation between specific positions (for that, use Orientation).
Default: 0, 0, 0

parameters.label: Width
parameters.description:
The width of the object (the size along the x-axis) in proportional layer coordinates.
Range: 1 to 1000; Default: 100

parameters.label: Height
parameters.description:
The height of the object (the size along the y-axis) in proportional layer coordinates.
Range: 1 to 1000; Default: 300

parameters.label: Depth
parameters.description:
The depth of the object (the size along the z-axis, away from the viewer, into the screen)  in proportional layer coordinates.
Range: 1 to 1000; Default: 100

parameters.label: Smoothing
parameters.description:
Smooths the edges and corners of the shape, resulting in round points and bevels. Use zero to render the shape without any smoothing, resulting in sharp edges and corners. Higher values specify the smoothing radius in the same coordinates used to define the size and position of the shape.
Range: 0 to 500; Default: 0

parameters.label: Position
parameters.description:
The position of the shape in 3D space in proportional layer coordinates.
Default: 0, 100, 600

parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description:
A scaling factor to apply to the 3D shape after it has been projected onto the 2D layer. This makes the shape larger or smaller without affecting the perspective in 3D space.
Range: 0 to 10; Default: 1

parameters.label: Shading
parameters.description:
Controls how the surface of the 3D shape is shaded.

Flat - Shades the shape without applying any lighting. The original colors in the layer are used as they are.
Point Light - Shades the shape as though it were lit with a point light. Point lights have a particular position in 3D space and emit light in all directions from that position. A light bulb is a point light.
Directional Light - Shades the shape as though it were lit with a directional light. Directional lights have a particular direction in 3D space, but the distance is not used. Direction lights are assumed to be infinitely distant. Directional lights are typically used for light sources that are very far away, like the sun. The difference between directional and point light sources is easiest to see in specular reflections on shiny surfaces.
Depth Map - Shades the shape in grays, where the color of each pixel is based on the distance from the viewer. Lighter pixels are closer to the viewer, while darker pixels are further into the screen. No lighting is applied to the shape.
Normal Map - Shades the shape using a normal map where the RGB values of each pixel encode the XYZ direction of a surface normal at that location on the surface of the layer. In other words, for each pixel, the values of red, green, and blue represent the X, Y, and Z values of a vector pointing outwards, perpendicular to the surface of the shape. The length of the encoded vector is always 1.
Normal & Depth Map - Shades the shape with a normal map multiplied by a depth map. In other words, the RGB values are encoded XYZ values of surface normal vectors for the shape, but the length of each vector is based on the distance from the viewer, rather than being normalized to a length of 1.

Default: Point Light

parameters.label: Intensity
parameters.description:
The brightness of the light source, used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directonal Light.
Range: 0% to 200%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Shine
parameters.description:
Controls how shiny the surface is. Higher values make the surface appear more metallic.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.8

parameters.label: Specularity
parameters.description:
The amount of specular reflectivity in the shape's surface material. Use lower values for a matte finish, and higher values for smooth, glossy surfaces.
Range: 1 to 150; Default: 30

parameters.label: Ambient
parameters.description:
The amount of ambient light in the environment. Ambient light is light that comes from all around, without a particular direction, and is useful for shapes in scenes with lots of soft, scattered light. Lower values result in less ambient light, for a harsher lighting effect and more pronounced shadows. Higher values result in less pronounced, weaker shadows.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.1

parameters.label: Color
parameters.description:
The color of the light source.
Default:

parameters.label: Direction
parameters.description:
The direction of the light source, used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directional Light.
Default: 75, -75

parameters.label: Elevation
parameters.description:
The distance of the light source from the shape (not from the screen). Positive values place the light source in front of the shape; negative values place the light source behind the shape. This is only used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directonal Light.
Range: -100 to 800; Default: 100

parameters.label: Mirror
parameters.description:
Flips alternate copies of the surface texture so that the edges meet seamlessly.
Default: off

parameters.label: Stretch
parameters.description:
Controls how textures are stretched when mapped to the surface of the shape.
When turned off, textures are mapped based on the size of the shape. For example, if the shape has a height of 300, the texture will be tiled 3 times vertically along the height of the shape. This is useful for textures that should repeat more as the shape gets larger (for example, stucco, wood grain, sand, and so on).
When turned on, textures are mapped so that one repetition of the texture exactly matches the size of the shape, which is useful if you want the texture to stretch or compress as the shape's size changes.
Note: The stretch setting is applied before Repeat X and Repeat Y, so for example, if stretch is turned on and Repeat Y is set to 2, there will always be 2 copies of the texture vertically along the hight of the shape, and those copies will stretch to match the height of the shape if it changes. Similarly if stretch is turned off and Repeat Y is set to 2, there will be two repetitions of the texture for each unit of height.
Default: off

parameters.label: Repeat X
parameters.description:
Number of times to repeat the layer texture horizontally.
Range: 0.01 to 20; Default: 1

parameters.label: Repeat Y
parameters.description:
Number of times to repeat the layer texture vertically.
Range: 0.01 to 20; Default: 1

parameters.label: Angle
parameters.description:
Rotates the surface texture of the shape (to rotate the individual texture tiles, use Twirl instead.)
Range: -3600º to 3600º; Default: 0º

parameters.label: Crop
parameters.description:
Controls cropping of the layer before it is used as a texture.
Values less than zero result in cropping of the layer, and values greater than zero add extra transparent space around the edges of the layer. Set this to zero for no cropping.
This is typically used to remove the transparent fringe or gap around layers that don't perfectly fill their bounding box, so that the layer tiles properly when used as a texture.
Range: 0.01 to 2; Default: 1

parameters.label: Twirl
parameters.description:
Rotates texture instances before tiling (which is different to Angle, which rotates the surface texture as a whole).
Range: -3600º to 3600º; Default: 0º

parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description:
Shifts the surface texture by the given amount. Use this to adjust the alignment of the texture on the surface of the object, or animate it for a moving surface.
Default: 0, 0

parameters.label: Precision
parameters.description:
Controls how precisely the 3D object is rendered by adjusting the number of iterations used when ray marching. More iterations will yield higher-quality results, especially for the edges of objects. Fewer iterations will give faster preview and export performance. For best results, a setting between 30 and 60 is recommended.
Range: 10 to 200; Default: 60

title: Box Blur
description: Blurs the layer by averaging the colors of nearby pixels in a surrounding rectangular area (hence "box" blur).

parameters.label: Strength
parameters.description:
The strength of the blur. Use higher values for a larger blur radius.
Range: 0 to 2; Default: 0.15

title: Brightness / Contrast
description: Adjusts the brightness or contrast of the layer.

parameters.label: Brightness
parameters.description:
Adjusts the overall lightness of the layer. Zero yields the original colors of the layer, unchanged. Positive values make the layer lighter, and negative values make the layer darker. +100% will give pure white, and -100% will give pure black, assuming no adjustment in contrast.
Range: -100% to +100%; Default: +0%

parameters.label: Contrast
parameters.description:
The separation of light and dark colors in a layer. Zero yields the original colors of the layer, unchanged. Positive values push light and dark colors further apart, increasing the contrast and making details in the midtones more obvious. Negative values pull the ligher and darker colors closer together towards a medium gray.
Range: -100% to +300%; Default: +0%

title: Bump Map
description: Uses a height map to model bumps or perturbations in the surface of the layer, then applies simulated lighting and shading.

parameters.label: Bump map
parameters.description:
Determines the height map to use to generate roughness or perturbations for the surface of the layer.

Layer Luminance - Uses the luminance of the layer; brighter pixels are higher and darker pixels are lower.
Background Luminance - Uses the luminance of the composite of everything behind the layer; brighter pixels are higher and darker pixels are lower.
Preset - Canvas - A built-in height map that resembles the texture of a painter's canvas.
Preset - Leather - A built-in height map that resembles leather.
Preset - Mud - A built-in height map that resembles mud dried or cracked mud.
Preset - Parchment - built-in height map that resembles crinkled paper or parchment.
Preset - Wood - A built-in height map that resembles wooden slats.
Preset - Weave - A built-in height map that resembles wicker or woven paper.

Default: Preset - Canvas

parameters.label: Fill
parameters.description:
Determines the colors for the surface of the layer before lighting and shading are applied.

Layer - The layer's original fill.
Background - The composite of everything behind the layer.
Color - A solid color, determined by Fill Color.

Default: Background

parameters.label: Fill Color
parameters.description:
The color used to fill the layer when Fill is set to Color.
Default:

parameters.label: Mirror
parameters.description:
Flips alternate copies of the bump map so that the edges meet seamlessly.
Default: off

parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description:
Scales the bump map relative to the layer. If using Layer Luminance or Background Luminance, a value of 1.0 will match the original exactly. When scaled down, the bump map will repeat.
Range: 0 to 10; Default: 1

parameters.label: Stretch
parameters.description:
Stretches the bump map. Negative values stretch the map vertically, while positive values stretch the map horizontally.
Default: 1, 1

parameters.label: Angle
parameters.description:
The angle of the bump map relative to the layer.
Range: -3600º to 3600º; Default: 0º

parameters.label: Crop
parameters.description:
For Layer Luminance or Background Luminance bump maps, controls cropping of the bump map before it is applied to the layer. This value is the proportion of the texture that is retained, while the rest is removed by cropping. For example, if this is set to 0.5, half of the texture (measured from the center) will be used, and the outer edges will be cropped. If this is greater than 1.0, the texture will shrink below its original size, leaving transparent areas around the edge.
Range: 0.01 to 2; Default: 1

parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description:
The amount to offset the bump map from its original position.
Default: 0, 0

parameters.label: Shading
parameters.description:
Controls how the surface of the layer is lit and shaded.

Point Light - Shades the shape as though it were lit with a point light. Point lights have a particular position in 3D space and emit light in all directions from that position. A light bulb is a point light.
Directional Light - Shades the shape as though it were lit with a directional light. Directional lights have a particular direction in 3D space, but the distance is not used. Direction lights are assumed to be infinitely distant. Directional lights are typically used for light sources that are very far away, like the sun. The difference between directional and point light sources is easiest to see in specular reflections on shiny surfaces.

Default: Directional Light

parameters.label: Intensity
parameters.description:
The brightness of the light source.
Range: 0% to 200%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Shine
parameters.description:
Controls how shiny the surface is. Higher values make the surface appear more metallic.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.8

parameters.label: Specularity
parameters.description:
The amount of specular reflectivity in the shape's surface material. Use lower values for a matte finish, and higher values for smooth, glossy surfaces.
Range: 1 to 150; Default: 30

parameters.label: Ambient
parameters.description:
The amount of ambient light in the environment. Ambient light is light that comes from all around, without a particular direction, and is useful for shapes in scenes with lots of soft, scattered light. Lower values result in less ambient light, for a harsher lighting effect and more pronounced shadows. Higher values result in less pronounced, weaker shadows.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.6

parameters.label: Color
parameters.description:
The color of the light source.
Default:

parameters.label: Shine Color
parameters.description:
The color of the highlights of the bump map.
Default:

parameters.label: Direction
parameters.description:
The direction of the light source, used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directional Light.
Default: 75, -75

parameters.label: Elevation
parameters.description:
The distance of the light source from the shape (not from the screen). Positive values place the light source in front of the shape; negative values place the light source behind the shape. This is only used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directonal Light.
Range: 0 to 1000; Default: 600

parameters.label: Height
parameters.description:
Height of the bumps in the map where the luminance is at maximum. Positive values cause the bumps to be raised above the surface of the layer, while negative values cause the bumps to be depressed into the surface of the layer.
Range: -100% to 100%; Default: 10%

parameters.label: Smoothing
parameters.description:
Smooths the surface normals calculated from the bump map. This is used to compensate for the appearance of steppiness due to the limited range of luminance values available for the bump map, or to remove unwated fine details, such as those caused by noise in a photograph.
Range: 1 to 10; Default: 1

title: CMYK Halftone Dots
description: Replaces continuous tones in the layer with a pattern of dots of varying sizes for each CMYK color channel, similar to the process used in magazine or comic book printing.

parameters.label: Amount
parameters.description:
The number of dots in the pattern horizontally. This is based on the width of the project. Increase for a finer pattern with more dots, decrease for a coarser patern with fewer dots.
Range: 0 to 500; Default: 100

parameters.label: Strength
parameters.description:
The sensity of the dot size to the luminance of the color in the original layer.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.5

parameters.label: Black
parameters.description:
The amount of blank ink used to represent equal parts of cyan, magenta, and yellow in the printing simulation. For the closest simulation of an actual printer, set this to 100%.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 0%

parameters.label: Angle
parameters.description:
The angle of the dot pattern in degrees clockwise.
Range: 0º to 3600º; Default: 45º

parameters.label: Phase
parameters.description:
The phase of alternate rows of dots in the pattern. Whole numbers cause all of the dots to line up in matching columns, and fractional numbers such as 0.5 can be used to stagger the dots. Typical halftone patterns have a phase of 0.5.
Range: -1000 to 1000; Default: 0.5

title: Channel Remap (HSV)
description: Remaps the hue, saturation, value, and alpha channels in the layer.

parameters.label: H
parameters.description:
Controls which input channel is mapped to the output hue channel.
Default: H

parameters.label: S
parameters.description:
Controls which input channel is mapped to the output saturation channel.
Default: S

parameters.label: V
parameters.description:
Controls which input channel is mapped to the output value (brightness) channel.
Default: V

parameters.label: A
parameters.description:
Controls which input channel is mapped to the output alpha (opacity) channel.
Default: A

title: Channel Remap (RGB)
description: Remaps the red, green, blue, and alpha channels in the layer.

parameters.label: R
parameters.description:
Controls the red channel output of the layer.
Default: R

parameters.label: G
parameters.description:
Controls the green channel output of the layer.
Default: G

parameters.label: B
parameters.description:
Controls the blue channel output of the layer.
Default: B

parameters.label: A
parameters.description:
Controls the alpha channel output of the layer. This determines the layer's opacity.
Default: A

title: Checker
description: Renders a checkerboard pattern in the opaque parts of the layer.

parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description:
The position of the pattern.
Default: 0, 0

parameters.label: Count
parameters.description:
The number of checkers along the vertical axis of the layer (the number of checkers along the horizontal axis is calculated automatically to obtain square checkers, except if Stretch has been applied.)
Range: 0.5 to 200; Default: 10

parameters.label: Stretch
parameters.description:
Stretches the checker pattern by scaling it independently along the x and y axes.
Default: 0, 0

parameters.label: Color
parameters.description:
The color of the checkerboard pattern (alternate checkers are transparent, revealing the original layer)
Default:

parameters.label: Punch Out
parameters.description:
When this is turned on, the color is ignored, and the checkers that would have been filled in punch holes through the layer instead.
Default: off

parameters.label: Angle
parameters.description:
The angle of the checker pattern in degrees clockwise
Range: 0º to 3600º; Default: 0º

parameters.label: Screen Space
parameters.description:
If turned off, the pattern is anchored to the layer's transform, and will move, scale and rotate with the layer. If turned on, the pattern is anchored to the screen regardless of the layer position, scale, or rotation (and the meaning of the Count property is based on the height of the composition rather than the height of the layer)
Default: off

title: Chroma Key
description: Keys out all pixels similar to a particular color, making those parts of the layer transparent. Useful for greenscreening or bluescreening.

parameters.label: Key Color
parameters.description:
The color to be keyed out. This color and similar colors will be made transparent based on the Threshold setting.
Default:

parameters.label: Threshold
parameters.description:
The amount by which the color of a pixel can differ from the Key Color and still be made transparent. Increase this to make a broader range of colors transparent. Note that the threshold is more sensitive to changes in hue and saturation (so you need higher thresholds to include more hues) and less sensitive to brightness, by a ratio of approximately 4:1.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.1

parameters.label: Feather
parameters.description:
The softness of the transition between transparent and visible pixels over the given threshold. Near zero, transparent areas have a hard edge. As this value increases, colors close to the Threshold will become partially transparent. This can result in a smoother edge, but can also result in more of the background or key color showing through, creatig a colored fringe around the layer. Use this together with Defringe for best results.
Range: 0.01 to 0.75; Default: 0.05

parameters.label: Defringe
parameters.description:
Enabling this causes Chroma Key to attempt to remove leftover tints in the Key Color from partially transparent pixels along the edge of the key.
Default: off

parameters.label: Invert
parameters.description:
Turn this on to reverse the effect and keep only the key color, while removing other colors
Default: off

title: Circular Ripple
description: Distorts the layer in a pattern concentric circles around a center point, similar to the ripples caused by a pebble dropped in water.

parameters.label: Center
parameters.description:
The center of the ripple pattern. 0,0 is the middle of the layer. Units are 1/1000 of the project width or height, measured from the layer center, regardless of the layer size or scale.
Default: 0, 0

parameters.label: Frequency
parameters.description:
The number of waves in the ripple pattern. Increase this for more waves spaced closer together; decrease it for fewer waves spaced further apart. The number of waves is based on the distance between the center of the project and the edge of the project, measured along the project's longest axis, regardless of the layer size or scale.
Range: 0 to 100; Default: 20

parameters.label: Strength
parameters.description:
The amount of distortion caused by the ripples as a ratio of the project size; use higher values for more distortion.
Range: -1 to 1; Default: 0.03

parameters.label: Phase
parameters.description:
Determines the offset within one wave cycle at which the ripples start at the center of the effect. Increasing this number causes ripples to contract towards the center; decreasing it causes them to expand away from the center. To make a seamless loop, make sure keyframe animaton starts and stops on Phase values with the same fraction (for example, from 0.5 to 4.5. or 0.0 to 1.0). Increasing this by 1 is exactly equivalent to the movement from one wave to the next within the ripple pattern.
Range: -1000 to 1000; Default: 0

parameters.label: Radius
parameters.description:
The distance that the ripple extends from the center as a ratio of the project's long dimension (0.5 will cause a ripple centered in the project to exactly touch the edges of the project).
Range: 0 to 0.8; Default: 0.3

parameters.label: Feather
parameters.description:
Controls how gradually the wave Strength falls off at the radius. A value of zero causes the waves to cut off abruptly, while higher values result in a more gradual fall-off.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.1

title: Clouds
description: Renders a procedurally generated cloud pattern in the opaque parts of the layer.

parameters.label: Parallax
parameters.description:
The difference in position of each additional stratum of the effect from the previous stratum. Animate this for parallax motion between the different strata of the effect. (This has no effect on the first stratum, or if Octaves is set to 1)
Default: 0, 0

parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description:
The X and Y offset of the pattern relative to the position of the layer.
Default: 0, 0

parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description:
The size of the pattern rendered in the layer.
Range: 0.01 to 50; Default: 1

parameters.label: Stretch
parameters.description:
Stretches the pattern by scaling it separately along the X and Y axes.
Default: 0, 0

parameters.label: Intensity
parameters.description:
Controls the amount of Color 2 used in the cloud pattern. With the default colors, increasing Intensity makes the whole effect brighter. Note that if this is greater than 0.5, there may be limiting or clipping in parts of the pattern.
Range: 0.25 to 0.75; Default: 0.5

parameters.label: Seed
parameters.description:
A seed used to generate the randomness used in this effect. Change this for different patterns of randomness.
Range: 0 to 5; Default: 0

parameters.label: Color 1
parameters.description:
One of two colors used to render the cloud pattern.
Default:

parameters.label: Color 2
parameters.description:
The other color used to render the cloud pattern (for best results, this color should contrast with Color 1)
Default:

parameters.label: Octaves
parameters.description:
The number of visual layers or strata in the effect. Use more octaves for a more complex, intricate pattern, or fewer octaves for a smoother pattern with less detail. Note that if this is set to 1, the Parallax property is ignored.
Range: 1 to 9; Default: 6

title: Color Temperature
description: Corrects a photo or video so it appears as if illuminated by a light source with a different color temperature. Useful for changing the mood of a scene or correcting for the wrong illumination or white balacne.

parameters.label: Temperature
parameters.description:
The target color temperature, in Kelvin, assuming the original image or video has a temperature of 6500K. Higher values represent a hotter black-body emitting light with a shorter wavelength (bluer or "cooler" light), while lower values represent a colder black-body emitting light with a longer wavelength (redder or "warmer" light).
Range: 1000K to 40000K; Default: 6500K

parameters.label: Strength
parameters.description:
The strength of the effect.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 100%

title: Color Tune
description: Smoothly tune the color of dark and light parts of a photo or video segmented by lift, gamma, and gain. Use this to achieve various color "looks", to change the mood of the video, or to match the color of different takes.

parameters.label: Lift
parameters.description:
Adjusts the color and lightness of the layer, affecting dark parts more strongly than light parts.
Default: 0.0º 0.0% +0.000

parameters.label: Gamma
parameters.description:
Adjusts the color and lightness of the layer, affecting midtones more strongly than light or dark parts.
Default: 0.0º 0.0% +0.000

parameters.label: Gain
parameters.description:
Adjusts the color and lightness of the layer, affecting light parts more strongly than dark parts.
Default: 0.0º 0.0% +0.000

parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description:
Adjusts the overall color and lightness of the layer.
Default: 0.0º 0.0% +0.000

title: Colorize
description: Changes the color of the layer, keeping the original luminance.

parameters.label: Tint
parameters.description:
The tint to apply to the layer. The angle determines the hue to apply, and the distance from the center of the disc determines how much is applied (none at the center, full strength at the edge).
Default: 0.0º 0.0%

title: Contour Gradient
description: Renders a gradient around the contours of the opaque parts of the layer.

parameters.label: Color 1
parameters.description:
The color of the edge of the gradient closest to the layer.
Default:

parameters.label: Color 2
parameters.description:
The color of the edge of the gradient furthest from the layer.
Default:

parameters.label: Width
parameters.description:
The width of the gradient. Higher values result in a gradient that spreads further away from the visible portion of the layer.
Range: 0 to 100; Default: 25

parameters.label: Outset
parameters.description:
The distance between the edges of the layer and the start of the gradient. Higher values result in a larger gap between the layer and the gradient.
Range: 0 to 150; Default: 0

parameters.label: Alpha
parameters.description:
The opacity of the gradient, independent of the layer fill.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Fill
parameters.description:
The opacity of the layer fill, independent of the gradient. Set this to 0% to show only the gradient, using the shape of the layer as a guide for the gradient but not showing the layer itself.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Fade
parameters.description:
When turned on, the gradient fades to transparent at its outer edge. When turned off, the gradient has a hard outer edge.
Default: off

title: Contour Lines
description: Draws one or more lines around the contours of the opaque parts of the layer.

parameters.label: Color
parameters.description:
The color of the lines.
Default:

parameters.label: Outset
parameters.description:
The distance between the layer and the innermost line.
Range: 0 to 150; Default: 10

parameters.label: Width
parameters.description:
The combined width of all the lines.
Range: 0 to 100; Default: 5

parameters.label: Alpha
parameters.description:
The opacity of the lines, independent of the layer fill.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Fill
parameters.description:
The opacity of the layer fill, independent of the lines. Set this to 0% to show only the contour lines, using the shape of the layer as a guide for the lines but not showing the layer itself.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Count
parameters.description:
The number of lines. Increasing this will result in thinner lines as they are squeezed into the space set for Width (to avoid this, also increase Width if you increase Count).
Range: 1 to 10; Default: 1

parameters.label: Phase
parameters.description:
An additional outset (negative) or inset (positive) for the lines. When you use this, lines pushed outside of the area defined by With loop back around to the inside and vice versa.
Range: -999 to 999; Default: 0

parameters.label: Fade
parameters.description:
When turned on, the lines fade to transparent the further they are away from the layer. When turned off, the lines appear solid.
Default: off

title: Contour Strips
description: Renders strips of thin paper-like material, layered front to back, using the layer luminance or opacity as a height map for each strip.

parameters.label: Count
parameters.description:
The number of strips to render.
Range: 0.5 to 200; Default: 10

parameters.label: Height
parameters.description:
The height of each strip.
Range: 0 to 15; Default: 1

parameters.label: Angle
parameters.description:
The angle of the strips. Use 0º for horizonta; strips and 90º for vertical strips.
Range: -3600º to 3600º; Default: 0º

parameters.label: Thickness
parameters.description:
The thickness of each individual strip. When this is set to zero, the strip has no thickness and Edge Color is ignored.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0

parameters.label: Phase
parameters.description:
An amount by which to shift each strip perpendicular to the Angle, as a ratio of the gap between the strips.
Range: -1000 to 1000; Default: 0

parameters.label: Invert
parameters.description:
Inverts the meaning of the Height Map so that areas that would normally result in taller strips will instead result in shorter, cutaway strips.
Default: off

parameters.label: Edge Color
parameters.description:
The color to use for the edges of the strips. This is applied only if the Thickness is greater than zero.
Default:

parameters.label: Color 1
parameters.description:
The color to use for the top of each strip.
Default:

parameters.label: Color 2
parameters.description:
The color to use for the bottom of each strip. If you don't want gradient strips, make sure this is the same as Color 1.
Default:

parameters.label: Light Color
parameters.description:
The color of the light source. This only applies if Thickness is greater than zero, in which case this color is used to light the edges of the strips based on the Light Angle.
Default:

parameters.label: Light Angle
parameters.description:
The angle of the light source used to light the edges of strips when the Thickness is greater than zero.
Range: 0º to 3600º; Default: 0º

parameters.label: Height Map
parameters.description:
The basis on which the height of each strip is determined at each pixel along the length of the strip.

Alpha: The height is based on the opacity of the layer; more opaque parts of the layer are taller; less opaque parts are shorter (unless Invert is turned on, in which case this is reversed).
Luminance: The height is based on the lightness of the layer; lighter parts of the layer are taller; darker parts are shorter (unless Invert is turned on, in which case this is reversed).

Default: Luminance

parameters.label: Mask To Layer
parameters.description:
Determines if the strip samples are masked to the original alpha (opacity) of the layer.

Off: The strips will fill the entire project or group canvas
On: The opacity of each pixel of a strip will match the opacity of the original layer where the strip was sampled

Default: on

title: Copy Background
description: Copies the background into the layer, effectively turning the layer into an adjustment layer. Effects applied to the layer after this will apply to the background pixels covered by the opaque parts of the layer.

parameters.label: Fill
parameters.description:
The amount of the original layer fill that remains visible. Leave this set to 0% to use the layer as an adjustment layer.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 0%

title: Count Up/Down
description: Replaces existing numbers in a text layer with the same number added to or multiplied with a fixed value. Useful for creating animations that count up or down.

parameters.label: Add
parameters.description:
This value is added to each original number in the text layer.
Range: -100000 to 100000; Default: 0

parameters.label: Multiply
parameters.description:
Each number in the original layer text is multiplied by this value.
Range: -2 to 2; Default: 1

title: Cube
description: Renders a faux-3D cube using the layer as a surface texture.

parameters.label: Orientation
parameters.description:
The orientation of the 3D object. This is similar to Rotation, but is interpreted differently when animated using Keyframes. /Orientation animates along the nearest path between different poses, and is useful for posing an object or animating an object between poses. Orientation values can't go out of range, so you can't use Orientation to "spin" an object (for that, use Orientation without animation to pose the object, and animate Rotation to create the spin)
Default: 0.000000,-0.000000,0.000000

parameters.label: Rotation
parameters.description:
The rotation of the 3D object. This is similar to Orientation, but the X, Y and Z components are interpreted independently when animated and can go out of range. This makes Rotation very handy for animating spinning motion, but not as convenient for creating arbitrary poses or animation between specific positions (for that, use Orientation).
Default: 0, 0, 0

parameters.label: Width
parameters.description:
The width of the cube as a proportion of the layer height.
Range: 0.01 to 10; Default: 1

parameters.label: Height
parameters.description:
The height of the cube as a proportion of the layer height.
Range: 0.01 to 10; Default: 1

parameters.label: Depth
parameters.description:
The depth of the cube (the size along the Z axis, away from the viewer, into the screen) as a proportion of the layer height.
Range: 0.01 to 10; Default: 1

parameters.label: Crop
parameters.description:
Controls cropping of the layer before it is used as a texture.
Values less than zero result in cropping of the layer, and values greater than zero add extra transparent space around the edges of the layer. Set this to zero for no cropping.
This is typically used to remove the transparent fringe or gap around layers that don't perfectly fill their bounding box, so that the layer tiles properly when used as a texture.
Range: 0.01 to 2; Default: 1

parameters.label: Position
parameters.description:
The position of the shape in 3D space in proportional layer coordinates.
Default: 0, 100, 600

parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description:
A scaling factor to apply to the 3D shape after it has been projected onto the 2D layer. This makes the shape larger or smaller without affecting the perspective in 3D space.
Range: 0 to 10; Default: 1

parameters.label: Shading
parameters.description:
Controls how the surface of the 3D shape is shaded.

Flat - Shades the shape without applying any lighting. The original colors in the layer are used as they are.
Point Light - Shades the shape as though it were lit with a point light. Point lights have a particular position in 3D space and emit light in all directions from that position. A light bulb is a point light.
Directional Light - Shades the shape as though it were lit with a directional light. Directional lights have a particular direction in 3D space, but the distance is not used. Direction lights are assumed to be infinitely distant. Directional lights are typically used for light sources that are very far away, like the sun. The difference between directional and point light sources is easiest to see in specular reflections on shiny surfaces.
Depth Map - Shades the shape in grays, where the color of each pixel is based on the distance from the viewer. Lighter pixels are closer to the viewer, while darker pixels are further into the screen. No lighting is applied to the shape.
Normal Map - Shades the shape using a normal map where the RGB values of each pixel encode the XYZ direction of a surface normal at that location on the surface of the layer. In other words, for each pixel, the values of red, green, and blue represent the X, Y, and Z values of a vector pointing outwards, perpendicular to the surface of the shape. The length of the encoded vector is always 1.
Normal & Depth Map - Shades the shape with a normal map multiplied by a depth map. In other words, the RGB values are encoded XYZ values of surface normal vectors for the shape, but the length of each vector is based on the distance from the viewer, rather than being normalized to a length of 1.

Default: Point Light

parameters.label: Intensity
parameters.description:
The brightness of the light source, used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directonal Light.
Range: 0% to 200%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Shine
parameters.description:
Controls how shiny the surface is. Higher values make the surface appear more metallic.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.8

parameters.label: Specularity
parameters.description:
The amount of specular reflectivity in the shape's surface material. Use lower values for a matte finish, and higher values for smooth, glossy surfaces.
Range: 1 to 150; Default: 30

parameters.label: Ambient
parameters.description:
The amount of ambient light in the environment. Ambient light is light that comes from all around, without a particular direction, and is useful for shapes in scenes with lots of soft, scattered light. Lower values result in less ambient light, for a harsher lighting effect and more pronounced shadows. Higher values result in less pronounced, weaker shadows.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.1

parameters.label: Color
parameters.description:
The color of the light source.
Default:

parameters.label: Direction
parameters.description:
The direction of the light source, used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directional Light.
Default: 75, -75

parameters.label: Elevation
parameters.description:
The distance of the light source from the shape (not from the screen). Positive values place the light source in front of the shape; negative values place the light source behind the shape. This is only used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directonal Light.
Range: -100 to 800; Default: 100

title: Curl
description: Deforms the layer by curling it into a circular shape from one end.

parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description:
The position of the virtual circle around which the layer is curled, from 0.0 (the left end of the layer) to 1.0 (the right end of the layer).
Range: -0.5 to 1.5; Default: 0.5

parameters.label: Radius
parameters.description:
The radius of the virtual circle around which the layer is curled.
Range: 0 to 50; Default: 1

parameters.label: Curl From
parameters.description:
The end of the layer that will be curled around the virtual circle.

Left - The left end of the layer will be curled around the circle; the right end will remain flat.
Right - The right end of the layer will be curled around the circle; the left end will remain flat.

Default: Right

parameters.label: Direction
parameters.description:
The placement of the virtual circle and the direction in which the layer curls.

Up - Places the virtual circle above the layer and curls the layer upward.
Down - Places the virtual circle below the layer and curls the layer downward.

Default: Up

parameters.label: Stretch
parameters.description:
Stretches or compresses the curled portion of the layer. When deforming a thick layer into a circle, the inside typically compresses while the outside expands. This setting gives your control over that. By tuning Stretch appropriately, for example, you can curl the layer so the inside remains constant and the outside expands more, or so the outside remains constant and the inside compresses more.
Range: 0.01 to 50; Default: 1

title: Cylinder
description: Renders a faux-3D cylinder using the layer as a surface texture.

parameters.label: Orientation
parameters.description:
The orientation of the 3D object. This is similar to Rotation, but is interpreted differently when animated using Keyframes. /Orientation animates along the nearest path between different poses, and is useful for posing an object or animating an object between poses. Orientation values can't go out of range, so you can't use Orientation to "spin" an object (for that, use Orientation without animation to pose the object, and animate Rotation to create the spin)
Default: 0.000000,-0.000000,0.000000

parameters.label: Rotation
parameters.description:
The rotation of the 3D object. This is similar to Orientation, but the X, Y and Z components are interpreted independently when animated and can go out of range. This makes Rotation very handy for animating spinning motion, but not as convenient for creating arbitrary poses or animation between specific positions (for that, use Orientation).
Default: 0, 0, 0

parameters.label: Radius
parameters.description:
The radius of the object in proportional layer coordinates.
Range: 1 to 1000; Default: 100

parameters.label: Length
parameters.description:
The length of the object (the size along the Y axis) in proportional layer coordinates.
Range: 1 to 1000; Default: 300

parameters.label: Smoothing
parameters.description:
Smooths the edges and corners of the shape, resulting in round points and bevels. Use zero to render the shape without any smoothing, resulting in sharp edges and corners. Higher values specify the smoothing radius in the same coordinates used to define the size and position of the shape.
Range: 0 to 5; Default: 0

parameters.label: Position
parameters.description:
The position of the shape in 3D space in proportional layer coordinates.
Default: 0, 100, 600

parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description:
A scaling factor to apply to the 3D shape after it has been projected onto the 2D layer. This makes the shape larger or smaller without affecting the perspective in 3D space.
Range: 0 to 10; Default: 1

parameters.label: Shading
parameters.description:
Controls how the surface of the 3D shape is shaded.

Flat - Shades the shape without applying any lighting. The original colors in the layer are used as they are.
Point Light - Shades the shape as though it were lit with a point light. Point lights have a particular position in 3D space and emit light in all directions from that position. A light bulb is a point light.
Directional Light - Shades the shape as though it were lit with a directional light. Directional lights have a particular direction in 3D space, but the distance is not used. Direction lights are assumed to be infinitely distant. Directional lights are typically used for light sources that are very far away, like the sun. The difference between directional and point light sources is easiest to see in specular reflections on shiny surfaces.
Depth Map - Shades the shape in grays, where the color of each pixel is based on the distance from the viewer. Lighter pixels are closer to the viewer, while darker pixels are further into the screen. No lighting is applied to the shape.
Normal Map - Shades the shape using a normal map where the RGB values of each pixel encode the XYZ direction of a surface normal at that location on the surface of the layer. In other words, for each pixel, the values of red, green, and blue represent the X, Y, and Z values of a vector pointing outwards, perpendicular to the surface of the shape. The length of the encoded vector is always 1.
Normal & Depth Map - Shades the shape with a normal map multiplied by a depth map. In other words, the RGB values are encoded XYZ values of surface normal vectors for the shape, but the length of each vector is based on the distance from the viewer, rather than being normalized to a length of 1.

Default: Point Light

parameters.label: Intensity
parameters.description:
The brightness of the light source, used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directonal Light.
Range: 0% to 200%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Shine
parameters.description:
Controls how shiny the surface is. Higher values make the surface appear more metallic.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.8

parameters.label: Specularity
parameters.description:
The amount of specular reflectivity in the shape's surface material. Use lower values for a matte finish, and higher values for smooth, glossy surfaces.
Range: 1 to 150; Default: 30

parameters.label: Ambient
parameters.description:
The amount of ambient light in the environment. Ambient light is light that comes from all around, without a particular direction, and is useful for shapes in scenes with lots of soft, scattered light. Lower values result in less ambient light, for a harsher lighting effect and more pronounced shadows. Higher values result in less pronounced, weaker shadows.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.1

parameters.label: Color
parameters.description:
The color of the light source.
Default:

parameters.label: Direction
parameters.description:
The direction of the light source, used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directional Light.
Default: 75, -75

parameters.label: Elevation
parameters.description:
The distance of the light source from the shape (not from the screen). Positive values place the light source in front of the shape; negative values place the light source behind the shape. This is only used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directonal Light.
Range: -100 to 800; Default: 100

parameters.label: Mirror
parameters.description:
Flips alternate copies of the surface texture so that the edges meet seamlessly.
Default: off

parameters.label: Repeat X
parameters.description:
Number of times to repeat the layer texture horizontally.
Range: 0.01 to 20; Default: 1

parameters.label: Repeat Y
parameters.description:
Number of times to repeat the layer texture vertically.
Range: 0.01 to 20; Default: 1

parameters.label: Angle
parameters.description:
Rotates the surface texture of the shape (to rotate the individual texture tiles, use Twirl instead.)
Range: -3600º to 3600º; Default: 0º

parameters.label: Crop
parameters.description:
Controls cropping of the layer before it is used as a texture.
Values less than zero result in cropping of the layer, and values greater than zero add extra transparent space around the edges of the layer. Set this to zero for no cropping.
This is typically used to remove the transparent fringe or gap around layers that don't perfectly fill their bounding box, so that the layer tiles properly when used as a texture.
Range: 0.01 to 2; Default: 1

parameters.label: Twirl
parameters.description:
Rotates texture instances before tiling (which is different to Angle, which rotates the surface texture as a whole).
Range: -3600º to 3600º; Default: 0º

parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description:
Shifts the surface texture by the given amount. Use this to adjust the alignment of the texture on the surface of the object, or animate it for a moving surface.
Default: 0, 0

parameters.label: Precision
parameters.description:
Controls how precisely the 3D object is rendered by adjusting the number of iterations used when ray marching. More iterations will yield higher-quality results, especially for the edges of objects. Fewer iterations will give faster preview and export performance. For best results, a setting between 30 and 60 is recommended.
Range: 10 to 200; Default: 60

title: Dark Glow
description: Creates dark blooms around darker areas within a photo or video layer based on a luminance threshold.

parameters.label: Diffusion
parameters.description:
Controls the softness of the glow as it spreads outwards from the dark parts of the layer.
Range: 0 to 2; Default: 0.25

parameters.label: Threshold
parameters.description:
A luminance threshold that controls which pixels emit the dark glow. Pixels with luminance under this threshold will have a dark glow; pixels with luminance over this threshold will not glow.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.3

parameters.label: Color
parameters.description:
A color to use instead of the original pixel color when rendering the glow.
Default:

parameters.label: Blend
parameters.description:
Controls the color of the glow. At 0% the original pixel color is used; at 100% the Color property is used. Values in between mix the original pixel color and glow color proportionally. The final mixed color is blended on top of the layer using the Multiply blending mode.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 25%

parameters.label: Alpha
parameters.description:
Controls the opacity of the glow.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 75%

title: Directional Blur
description: Blurs the layer by averaging nearby pixel colors along a specified angle, similar to the blur caused by motion in a fixed direction.

parameters.label: Strength
parameters.description:
The strength of the blur (use zero for no blur)
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.15

parameters.label: Angle
parameters.description:
The angle of the blur (0º is horizontal)
Range: 0º to 3600º; Default: 0º

title: Displacement Map
description: Displaces pixels in the background based on the color of pixels in the layer.

parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description:
The maximum amount of displacement of each pixel in the background. This may be reduced depending on the color of pixels in the map layer.
Default: 0.000000,0.000000

parameters.label: X
parameters.description:
Selects whether Luminance Red, Green, or Blue channels will be used to calculate displacement along the X axis.
Default: R

parameters.label: Y
parameters.description:
Selects whether Luminance Red, Green, or Blue channels will be used to calculate displacement along the Y axis.
Default: G

parameters.label: Edges
parameters.description:
Controls what happens when the displacement makes background pixels that would normally be outside of the screen visible.

Repeat - Pixels on the edge of the screen are treated as though they are repeated indefinitely beyond the edges of the screen, resulting in streaks.
Mirror - Repeats the contents of the screen in an infinite pattern of tiles, mirroring alternate copies.
Clear - The area outside the screen is considered transparent.
Tile - Repeats the contents of the screen in an infinite pattern of tiles.

Default: Mirror

parameters.label: From Center
parameters.description:
Changes the displacement behavior so that lighter pixels in the map layer shift in direction of the given /Offset, while darker pixels shift in the opposite direction. Pixels with exactly 50% intensity in the relevant color channel cause no shift at all.
Default: off

parameters.label: Invert
parameters.description:
Reverses the effects of light and dark pixels in the map layer.
Default: off

title: Dissolve
description: Randomly removes pixels in the layer. This effect can be animated to create a pixel dissolve transition.

parameters.label: Strength
parameters.description:
The ratio of pixels to be removed. At 0, no pixels are removed. At 1, all pixels are removed.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.75

parameters.label: Seed
parameters.description:
A value used to generate the random patterns of removed pixels. Animate this for a transparent static-like result.
Range: 0 to 5; Default: 0

title: Dots
description: Draws a pattern of random dots in the opaque parts of the layer. Each dot is placed randomly, but the randomness is controlled so that the overall distribution of dots when taken together is approximately uniform.

parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description:
The scale of dot pattern as a whole.
Range: 0.01 to 100; Default: 1

parameters.label: Phase
parameters.description:
The phase of each dot within its movement cycle. Dots repeat their cycle at whole number intervals, which can be useful for looping animations.
Range: -99 to 99; Default: 0

parameters.label: Radius
parameters.description:
The size of each individual dot. Generally it is best to use values between 0 and 1, and values greater than 1 can result in clipping (however, sometimes clipping may be desired for stylistic reasons)
Range: 0 to 2; Default: 0.2

parameters.label: Feather
parameters.description:
Softens the edges of the individual dots.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.1

parameters.label: Color 1
parameters.description:
Randomly blended with Color 2 to determine the color of each dot. To make all the dots the same color, use the same color for Color 1 and Color 2.
Default:

parameters.label: Color 2
parameters.description:
Randomly blended with Color 1 to determine the color of each dot.
Default:

parameters.label: Density
parameters.description:
The density of the dot pattern. With a density of 11, there is one dot per cell in the pattern (see below). For densities below 11, some are rendered without any dots, and with densities over 11, multiple dots are rendered per cell.
Range: 1 to 13; Default: 12

parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description:
Relative offset of the dot pattern from the layer.
Default: 0, 0

parameters.label: Fill
parameters.description:
Controls how much of the original layer fill is visible in the layer. At 100%, the dots are simply overlaid on top of the layer. At 0% the layer fill is removed entirely, and merely used as a mask for the dot pattern.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Alpha
parameters.description:
Controls the opacity of the dot pattern from transparent (0%) to opaque (100%)
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Blending Mode
parameters.description:
Controls how the dots are blended with the layer fill.

Normal
Multiply
Screen

Default: Normal

parameters.label: Seed
parameters.description:
This value is used to generate the random pattern of dots.
Range: 0 to 5; Default: 0

parameters.label: Slack
parameters.description:
Controls how far away from the center of each cell the dot can move. Set this to 0% to place each dot in the middle of its cell, essentially removing any random placement from the dot pattern (colors and density are still randomized, however.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 100%

title: Drawing Progress
description: Clips the beginning and end of the vector outline of the layer so that the outline is only partially drawn.

parameters.label: Start
parameters.description:
The percentage of the vector outline to be clipped out, measured from the beginning of the outline
Range: 0 to 100; Default: 0

parameters.label: End
parameters.description:
The percentage of the vector outline to be clipped out, measured from the end of the outline
Range: 0 to 100; Default: 100

title: Echo Keyframes
description: Creates one or more copies or "echoes" of the layer as it appeared or will appear at different points in time, based only on keyframe animation applied to the layer.

parameters.label: Seconds
parameters.description:
Controls how far away in time the most distant echo is. For positive numbers, the most distant echo is in the past (so it's the earliest echo) but you can also use negative numbers here to echo future animations that haven't happened yet (in which case, this would be the latest echo).
Range: -60 to 60; Default: 0.5

parameters.label: Count
parameters.description:
The number of echoes between the current time and the final echo. For example, if Seconds is set to 1, and Count is set to 2, there will be two echoes: One half a second before the current time, and the other a whole second before the current time.
Range: 0 to 20; Default: 1

parameters.label: Alpha
parameters.description:
The alpha (opacity) of the final echo. This is also partially applied to intermediate echoes, causing echoes to appear to fade out gradually.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 1

parameters.label: Composite
parameters.description:
Controls whether the echoes are drawn behind or on top of the layer.

Atop: Echoes appear on top of the layer, covering it up. The most distant echo is drawn at the very top.
Behind: Echoes appear behind the layer, and are covered by it. The most distant echo is drawn at the very back.

Default: Behind

title: Edge Glow
description: Detects edges in a photo or video and creates a glow around them.

parameters.label: Smoothing
parameters.description:
Smoothing factor applied when detecting edges. Decrease this for more detail, increase this for less detail.
Range: 1 to 10; Default: 5

parameters.label: Threshold
parameters.description:
Controls the sensitivity of the edge detection. Increase this for fewer edges, decrease for more edges.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.7

parameters.label: Invert
parameters.description:
Turn this on to invert edge detection (glows will come from areas without edges). This works best with high values of Smoothing or low values of Threshold.
Default: off

parameters.label: Blur
parameters.description:
Controls how soft the glow is and far it extends from edges.
Range: 0 to 2; Default: 0.1

parameters.label: Spread
parameters.description:
Controls the size of the solid core of the glow. Higher values result in a core that spreads out further from each edge before falling off.
Range: 0 to 3; Default: 1.5

parameters.label: Color
parameters.description:
A color to use instead of the original pixel color when rendering the glow.
Default:

parameters.label: Blend
parameters.description:
Controls the color of the glow. At 0% the original pixel color is used; at 100% the Color property is used. Values in between mix the original pixel color and glow color proportionally. The final mixed color is blended on top of the layer using the Screen blending mode.
Range: 0% to 200%; Default: 0%

parameters.label: Gamma
parameters.description:
A gamma correction to apply to the original layer content before compositing the glow into the layer. This can be useful for accenting the glowing edges by making the rest of the layer darker.
Range: 0.01 to 9.99; Default: 1

parameters.label: Brightness
parameters.description:
A linear brightness adjustment to apply to the original layer content before compositing the glow into the layer. This can be useful for accenting the glowing edges by making the rest of the layer darker.
Range: -100% to +100%; Default: +0%

parameters.label: Tint Color
parameters.description:
A color to be overlaid on top of the original layer before compositing the glow into the layer.
Default:

parameters.label: Tint
parameters.description:
The amount of the Tint Color that is composited into the layer. At 0% the tint color is not visible, and at 100% the tint color completely covers the layer, so that only the glowing edges are visible.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 0%

title: Electric Edges
description: Creates a glowing energy-like outline and optional contour lines around the opaque parts of the layer, which can be animated to look like arcs of electricity.

parameters.label: Distance
parameters.description:
The distance of the outline from the opaque parts of the layer. When this is 0.0, the outline will hug the layer tightly. When this is 1.0 the outline will be most distant from the layer. The meaning of 1.0 is somewhat arbitrary and due to limitations of the algorithm used for Electric Edges. The maximum distance may be be increased beyond 1.0 in the future.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.3

parameters.label: Thickness
parameters.description:
The thickness of the outline. A value of 0.0 will yield the thinnest possible outline (about 1 pixel at 540p; thicker at higher resolutions).  value of 1.0 will yield a thickness about half of the maximum Distance.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.8

parameters.label: Detail
parameters.description:
The amount of complexity or detail in the distortions in the outline. A value of 0.0 will result in a smooth outline (and will cause other properties such as Seed, Evolution, and Distortion to be effectively ignored). A value of 20.0 will result in such fine detail that the edge effectively becomes a point cloud, looking more like pixel noise than electricity. Typical useful values are in the 1.0 to 5.0 range.
Range: 0 to 20; Default: 2

parameters.label: Distortion
parameters.description:
Controls how strongly the outline is warped. Positive values warp the outline outwards and negative values warp the outline inwards. A value of 0.0 will result in a smooth outline (and will cause other properties such as Seed, Complexity, and Distortion to be effectively ignored). By combining negative distortion with a distance of zero, it's possible to create an outline that is inset within the bounds of the layer.
Range: -1 to 1; Default: 0.5

parameters.label: Color
parameters.description:
The core color used for the edge. This is blended additively with any glow color, so for example, if Color is pure red, and Glow Color is pure green, the overlapping regions will be yellow (the mixture of green and red light).
Default:

parameters.label: Glow Color
parameters.description:
The color used for a soft glow that surrounds the edge. The amount of glow that appears is controlled by the Glow property (if Glow is zero, Glow Color is effectively ignored). Glow Color is blended additively with Color, so for example, if Color is pure red, and Glow Color is pure green, the overlapping regions will be yellow (the mixture of green and red light).
Default:

parameters.label: Alpha
parameters.description:
Controls the opacity of the core outline, independently of the glow opacity. Setting this to 0.0 will hide the outline, but the glow will remain.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Glow
parameters.description:
Controls the opacity of the soft glow surrounding the outline, independently of the outline opacity. Setting this to 0.0 will hide the glow, but the outline will remain.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 50%

parameters.label: Seed
parameters.description:
A value used to generate the pattern of distortions in the outline, glow, and (if enabled) contours. Change this for a different pattern of distortions. Use keyframe animation to make the distortions in the outline change radically every frame (useful for simulating an electric look).
Range: 0 to 5; Default: 0

parameters.label: Evolution
parameters.description:
Controls small, gradual changes in the random pattern of distortions in the outline, glow, and (if enabled) contours. Animate this to produce flowing motion (useful for simulating an energy plasma look, for example).
Range: -999 to 999; Default: 0

parameters.label: Fill
parameters.description:
Controls how opaque the layer is, independent of the outline or glow. Set this to 0.0 to hide the layer entirely, leaving only the outline and glow visible.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Strength
parameters.description:
Controls the strength of the random mask used to hide parts of the outline and glow. When the strength is 0.0, the mask will not be used, and the outline and glow will be completely visible. At a strength of 1.0, the glow and outline will be completely masked and will disappear.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0

parameters.label: Evolution
parameters.description:
Controls small, gradual changes in the random pattern of the mask.
Range: -50 to 50; Default: 0

parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description:
Adjusts the size of the random patterns used to generate the mask. Smaller values result in smaller holes in the mask, spaced closer together; larger values result in larger holes spaced further apart.
Range: 0.01 to 50; Default: 1

parameters.label: Seed
parameters.description:
A random seed used to generate the mask. Change this for different patterns of holes in the mask.
Range: 0 to 5; Default: 0

parameters.label: Color
parameters.description:
A color that replaces the outline and glow colors in areas where the mask is partly transparent.
Default:

parameters.label: Render Contours
parameters.description:
When enabled, a series of contour lines will be drawn in front of the opaque parts of the layer, distorted based on the distance from the edge of the layer.
Default: off

parameters.label: Count
parameters.description:
The number of contour lines drawn, based on the natural width of the layer (this may differ from the actual number of lines visible, due to scaling, rotation, or other transforms on the layer, or due to the combination of contour angle and layer aspect ratio).
Range: 0 to 200; Default: 20

parameters.label: Phase
parameters.description:
An offset applied to the contour lines, perpendicular to the angle of the lines.
Range: -999 to 999; Default: 0

parameters.label: Angle
parameters.description:
The angle of the contour lines. A angle of 0º results in horizontal lines, with increasing angles rotating the lines counter-clockwise.
Range: -3600º to 3600º; Default: 0º

title: Ellipsoid
description: Renders a faux-3D ellipsoid using the layer as a surface texture.

parameters.label: Orientation
parameters.description:
The orientation of the 3D object. This is similar to Rotation, but is interpreted differently when animated using Keyframes. /Orientation animates along the nearest path between different poses, and is useful for posing an object or animating an object between poses. Orientation values can't go out of range, so you can't use Orientation to "spin" an object (for that, use Orientation without animation to pose the object, and animate Rotation to create the spin)
Default: 0.000000,-0.000000,0.000000

parameters.label: Rotation
parameters.description:
The rotation of the 3D object. This is similar to Orientation, but the X, Y and Z components are interpreted independently when animated and can go out of range. This makes Rotation very handy for animating spinning motion, but not as convenient for creating arbitrary poses or animation between specific positions (for that, use Orientation).
Default: 0, 0, 0

parameters.label: Radius
parameters.description:
The largest radius of the ellipsoid (if Squeeze and Flatten are not used, the ellipsoid is a perfect sphere, and this is its radius)
Range: 1 to 500; Default: 250

parameters.label: Squeeze
parameters.description:
Reducing this squeezes the ellipsoid horizontally along its X axis, making it narrower. The value is a percentage of Radius.
Range: 25% to 100%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Flatten
parameters.description:
Reducing this flattens the ellipsoid vertically along its Y axis, making it shorter. The value is a percentage of Radius.
Range: 25% to 100%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Position
parameters.description:
The position of the shape in 3D space in proportional layer coordinates.
Default: 0, -20, 550

parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description:
A scaling factor to apply to the 3D shape after it has been projected onto the 2D layer. This makes the shape larger or smaller without affecting the perspective in 3D space.
Range: 0 to 10; Default: 1

parameters.label: Shading
parameters.description:
Controls how the surface of the 3D shape is shaded.

Flat - Shades the shape without applying any lighting. The original colors in the layer are used as they are.
Point Light - Shades the shape as though it were lit with a point light. Point lights have a particular position in 3D space and emit light in all directions from that position. A light bulb is a point light.
Directional Light - Shades the shape as though it were lit with a directional light. Directional lights have a particular direction in 3D space, but the distance is not used. Direction lights are assumed to be infinitely distant. Directional lights are typically used for light sources that are very far away, like the sun. The difference between directional and point light sources is easiest to see in specular reflections on shiny surfaces.
Depth Map - Shades the shape in grays, where the color of each pixel is based on the distance from the viewer. Lighter pixels are closer to the viewer, while darker pixels are further into the screen. No lighting is applied to the shape.
Normal Map - Shades the shape using a normal map where the RGB values of each pixel encode the XYZ direction of a surface normal at that location on the surface of the layer. In other words, for each pixel, the values of red, green, and blue represent the X, Y, and Z values of a vector pointing outwards, perpendicular to the surface of the shape. The length of the encoded vector is always 1.
Normal & Depth Map - Shades the shape with a normal map multiplied by a depth map. In other words, the RGB values are encoded XYZ values of surface normal vectors for the shape, but the length of each vector is based on the distance from the viewer, rather than being normalized to a length of 1.

Default: Point Light

parameters.label: Intensity
parameters.description:
The brightness of the light source, used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directonal Light.
Range: 0% to 200%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Shine
parameters.description:
Controls how shiny the surface is. Higher values make the surface appear more metallic.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.8

parameters.label: Specularity
parameters.description:
The amount of specular reflectivity in the shape's surface material. Use lower values for a matte finish, and higher values for smooth, glossy surfaces.
Range: 1 to 150; Default: 30

parameters.label: Ambient
parameters.description:
The amount of ambient light in the environment. Ambient light is light that comes from all around, without a particular direction, and is useful for shapes in scenes with lots of soft, scattered light. Lower values result in less ambient light, for a harsher lighting effect and more pronounced shadows. Higher values result in less pronounced, weaker shadows.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.1

parameters.label: Color
parameters.description:
The color of the light source.
Default:

parameters.label: Direction
parameters.description:
The direction of the light source, used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directional Light.
Default: 75, -75

parameters.label: Elevation
parameters.description:
The distance of the light source from the shape (not from the screen). Positive values place the light source in front of the shape; negative values place the light source behind the shape. This is only used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directonal Light.
Range: -100 to 800; Default: 100

parameters.label: Mirror
parameters.description:
Flips alternate copies of the surface texture so that the edges meet seamlessly.
Default: off

parameters.label: Repeat X
parameters.description:
Number of times to repeat the layer texture horizontally.
Range: 0.01 to 20; Default: 1

parameters.label: Repeat Y
parameters.description:
