Number of times to repeat the layer texture vertically.
Range: 0.01 to 20; Default: 1

parameters.label: Angle
parameters.description:
Rotates the surface texture of the shape (to rotate the individual texture tiles, use Twirl instead.)
Range: -3600º to 3600º; Default: 0º

parameters.label: Crop
parameters.description:
Controls cropping of the layer before it is used as a texture.
Values less than zero result in cropping of the layer, and values greater than zero add extra transparent space around the edges of the layer. Set this to zero for no cropping.
This is typically used to remove the transparent fringe or gap around layers that don't perfectly fill their bounding box, so that the layer tiles properly when used as a texture.
Range: 0.01 to 2; Default: 1

parameters.label: Twirl
parameters.description:
Rotates texture instances before tiling (which is different to Angle, which rotates the surface texture as a whole).
Range: -3600º to 3600º; Default: 0º

parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description:
Shifts the surface texture by the given amount. Use this to adjust the alignment of the texture on the surface of the object, or animate it for a moving surface.
Default: 0, 0

parameters.label: Precision
parameters.description:
Controls how precisely the 3D object is rendered by adjusting the number of iterations used when ray marching. More iterations will yield higher-quality results, especially for the edges of objects. Fewer iterations will give faster preview and export performance. For best results, a setting between 30 and 60 is recommended.
Range: 10 to 200; Default: 60

title: Exposure / Gamma
description: Adjusts the exposure, gamma curve, and luminance offset of photo and video layers.

parameters.label: Exposure
parameters.description:
Adjusts the exposure of a photo or video layer, making it appear as if more or less light had been captured by the camera. Increasing or decreasing the exposure primarily affects the brighter parts of the layer.
Range: -2 to 2; Default: 0

parameters.label: Gamma
parameters.description:
Applies a gamma correction to the layer based on a power function.
Range: 0.01 to 9.99; Default: 1

parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description:
Makes all pixels in the layer brighter or darker by adding the offset value to the RGB values of the pixels.
Range: -0.9 to 0.9; Default: 0

title: Fade In/Out
description: Automatically animates the layer opacity to fade the layer in at its beginning, and out again at its end.

parameters.label: In
parameters.description:
Duration of the fade-in at the beginning of the layer, in seconds.
Range: 0s to 10s; Default: 0.5s

parameters.label: Out
parameters.description:
Duration of the fade-out at the end of the layer, in seconds.
Range: 0s to 10s; Default: 0.5s

title: Feather
description: Softens the edges of the opaque part of the layer by blurring the alpha channel.

parameters.label: Strength
parameters.description:
The strength of the blur used to soften the layer edges. Higher values yield softer edges.
Range: 0 to 2; Default: 0.15

parameters.label: Iterations
parameters.description:
The number of box blur iterations used to blur the alpha channel. This affects the quality of the feather. More iterations give better quality, but slower preview performance. Three iterations are recommended.
Range: 1 to 5; Default: 3

title: Fill Behind
description: Fills the area behind the layer with a modified copy of the layer, with optional adjustments such as blur, saturation, tint, and brightness, providing a convenient way to quickly adapt a photo or video for use in a project with a different aspect ratio or orientation.

parameters.label: Strength
parameters.description:
The strength of the blur used to blur the background copy of the layer. Increasing this also zooms in the background copy slightly to avoid a transparent fringe forming around the edges of the screen where the blur ends.
Range: 0 to 2; Default: 0.3

parameters.label: Feather
parameters.description:
The amount of feathering around the edges of the foreground (original layer) as a proportion of the blur Strength (this is ignored if Strength is zero).
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.05

parameters.label: Saturation
parameters.description:
Controls the amount of color in the background copy of the layer. Negative values remove color, with -100% resulting in a grayscale background. Positive values boost the colorfulness any existing colors in the background. Use zero for the original color.
Range: -100% to +100%; Default: +0%

parameters.label: Brightness
parameters.description:
Controls the brightness of the background copy of the layer. Use zero for the original brightness. Negative values make background darker, and positive values make it lighter.
Range: -100% to +100%; Default: +0%

parameters.label: Gamma
parameters.description:
Apples a gamma correction to the luminance of the background copy of the layer.
Range: 0.01 to 9.99; Default: 1

parameters.label: BG Alpha
parameters.description:
The opacity of the foreground (original) copy of the layer.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: FG Alpha
parameters.description:
The opacity of the background copy of the layer.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Tint Color
parameters.description:
A tint color to be applied to the background copy of the layer, based on the setting of Tint. Only the hue and saturation of the tint color are used; the original luminance is preserved.
Default:

parameters.label: Tint
parameters.description:
The amount of Tint Color to apply to the background copy of the layer.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 0%

parameters.label: Edge Color
parameters.description:
A color to apply around the edges of the foreground (original layer), based on the setting of Edge Strength. This is a soft border, the thickness of which is determined by the blur Strength, so this does nothing when Strength is zero.
Default:

parameters.label: Edge Strength
parameters.description:
The opacity of the soft edge drawn around the foreground (original layer) using Edge Color. If this is zero, no edge is drawn. At 100% the innermost pixels of the edge are fully opaque, while the outer ones are transparent. The edge thickness is based on the blur Strength, so when Strength is zero, this is ignored.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 0%

parameters.label: Scan Line Strength
parameters.description:
The opacity of gaps or dark areas between optional CRT-style scanlines rendered on top of the background copy of the layer. Set this to zero to disable scanline simulation.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 0%

parameters.label: Scan Line Count
parameters.description:
The number of scanlines to render, when Scan Line Strength isn't zero.
Range: 100 to 2160; Default: 720

parameters.label: FG Offset
parameters.description:
Shifts the position of the foreground (original layer) independently of the background.
Default: 0, 0

parameters.label: FG Scale
parameters.description:
Controls the size of the foreground (original layer) independently of the background.
Range: 0.01 to 100; Default: 1

parameters.label: BG Offset
parameters.description:
Shifts the position of the background copy of the layer independently of the foreground.
Default: 0, 0

parameters.label: BG Scale
parameters.description:
Controls the size of the background copy of the layer independently of the foreground.
Range: 0.01 to 100; Default: 1

title: Find Edges
description: Finds edges in a photo or video layer by detecting sharp changes in luminance between nearby pixels.

title: Flicker
description: Randomly flickers the layer by automatically adjusting the opacity over time.

title: Flip Layer
description: Flips the layer in 3D along a specified axis.

title: Four-color Gradient
description: Overlays the opaque parts of the layer with a gradient made between four individually positioned colors.

title: Fractal Ridges
description: Renders a procedurally generated pattern of turbulent ridges in the opaque parts of the layer.

title: Fractal Warp
description: Distorts the layer using a procedurally generated fractal pattern. Useful for simulating distortions caused by real-world phenomena such as fire, water, and heat.

title: Gaussian Blur
description: Applies a fast, smooth blur to the layer using a gaussian function, reducing noise and details in the layer.

title: Glass
description: Renders the layer as if the opaque parts were made of beveled glass, refracting the background and overlaying specular highlights.

title: Glow
description: Draws a glow around the outside of the opaque parts of the layer.

title: Glow Scan
description: Renders a blur combined with a smooth glow within a region of the layer defined by a luminance band.

title: Gradient Map
description: Maps colors in the layer onto a gradient based on their lightness.

title: Gradient Overlay
description: Overlays the opaque parts of the layer with a gradient.

title: Grid
description: Overlays the opaque parts of the layer with grid lines.

title: Grid Repeat
description: Dynamically generates multiple copies of the layer, arranged in a grid.

title: Halftone Dots
description: Replaces continuous tones in the layer with a pattern of monochrome dots of varying sizes, similar to the process used in newspaper or comic book printing.

parameters.label: Amount
parameters.description:
The number of dots in the pattern horizontally. This is based on the width of the project. Increase for a finer pattern with more dots, decrease for a coarser patern with fewer dots.
Range: 0 to 500; Default: 100

parameters.label: Strength
parameters.description:
The sensity of the dot size to the luminance of the color in the original layer.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.50

parameters.label: Angle
parameters.description:
The angle of the dot pattern in degrees clockwise.
Range: 0º to 3600º; Default: 45º

parameters.label: Phase
parameters.description:
The phase of alternate rows of dots in the pattern. Whole numbers cause all of the dots to line up in matching columns, and fractional numbers such as 0.5 can be used to stagger the dots. Typical halftone patterns have a phase of 0.5.
Range: -1000 to 1000; Default: 0.50

parameters.label: Color 1
parameters.description:
The color of the dots. Use a color darker than the background for realistic results.
Default:

parameters.label: Color 2
parameters.description:
The color of the background. Use a color lighter than the dots for realistic results.
Default:

title: Halftone Lines
description: Replaces continuous tones in the layer with a pattern of monochrome lines of varying thickness, a variation of the process used in newspaper or comic book printing.

parameters.label: Amount
parameters.description:
The number of lines in the pattern horizontally. This is based on the width of the project. Increase for a finer pattern with more lines, decrease for a coarser patern with fewer lines.
Range: 0 to 500; Default: 100

parameters.label: Strength
parameters.description:
The sensity of the line width to the luminance of the color in the original layer.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.50

parameters.label: Angle
parameters.description:
The angle of the lines in degrees clockwise (use 0º for horizontal lines).
Range: 0º to 3600º; Default: 45º

parameters.label: Phase
parameters.description:
The phase of lines in the pattern. Changing this shifts the lines in a direction perpendicular to the Angle. Whole numbers of phase such as 1.0, 2.0 and so on represent the same pattern, so start and end with the same fractional part if you want to make a loopable animation.
Range: -1000 to 1000; Default: 0

parameters.label: Color 1
parameters.description:
The color of the lines. Use a color darker than the background for realistic results.
Default:

parameters.label: Color 2
parameters.description:
The color of the background. Use a color lighter than the lines for realistic results.
Default:

title: Heart
description: Renders a faux-3D heart using the layer as a surface texture.

title: Hexagon Array
description: Covers opaque parts of the layer in an array of hexagons.

parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description:
The offset of the hexagon pattern relative to the layer position.
Default: 0, 0

parameters.label: Size
parameters.description:
The size of the pattern, based on the height of the layer (at a size of 100, approximately one hexagon fills the layer vertically)
Range: 0.5 to 200; Default: 10

parameters.label: Stretch
parameters.description:
Scales the hexagon pattern vertically, independent of the overall size, which can be used to stretch or squash the hexagons in the pattern.
Range: 0.01 to 10; Default: 1

parameters.label: Coloring
parameters.description:
Controls how hexagons in the pattern are colored.

Layer - Each hexagon is filled with the color of the layer using the pixel at the center of the hexagon.
Solid - All of the hexagons are filled with Color 1.
Stripes - The hexagons are filled with Color 1 and Color 2 in alternating rows.
Tricolor - The hexagons are colored using all three colors in a uniform tiling where no two adjacent hexagons have the same color, and colors repeat every three hexagons.
Cube - Each hexagon is divided into three rhombi, each shaded with one of the three colors, so that no two adjacent rhombi use the same color. With the appropriate choice of colors, hexagons appear as cubes seen from the corner. If Line Width is zero, this is a rhombille tiling or dice lattice.
Triangles - Each hexagon is divided into six triangles, which are shaded with all three colors such that no two adjacent triangles have the same color. Color 1 is used the most, Color 2 second most, and Color 3 the least. For a triangle coloring with only two colors where no two adjacent triangles share a color, set Color 2 and Color 3 to the same color, and make Color 1 different.
Pentagonal - Each hexagon is divided into three irregular pentagons, each colored using all three colors such that no two adjacent pentagons share a color. With a Line Width of zero, this is an isohedral type 3 pentagonal tiling.

Default: Solid

parameters.label: Color 1
parameters.description:
The first color, used in every coloring mode except Layer.
Default:

parameters.label: Color 2
parameters.description:
The second color, used in every coloring mode except Layer and Solid.
Default:

parameters.label: Color 3
parameters.description:
The third color, used in Tricolor, Cubes, Triangles and Pentagonal.
Default:

parameters.label: Alpha
parameters.description:
Controls the opacity of the hexagonal array pattern.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Blending Mode
parameters.description:
Controls how the hexagonal array pattern is blended with the layer.

Normal - The pattern is overlaid normally on top of the layer, but only within the layer's original opaque areas.
Multiply - The standard Multiply blending mode.
Screen - The standard Screen blending mode.
Overlay - The standard Overlay blending mode.
Overflow - The pattern is overlaid normally on top of the layer, but hexagons that would normally be partially cut off at the edge of the layer are instead rendered fully.
Punch Out - Hexagon punch through the layer, making the layer transparent and leaving only the gaps between the hexagons (use this with a Line Width greater than zero).

Default: Normal

parameters.label: Scale Using Layer Alpha
parameters.description:
If this is turned on, each hexagon will be scaled based on the alpha value of the layer at the pixel at the center of the hexagon. For example, if the pixel under the hexagon is only 50% opaque, the hexagon will be only 50% of the size defined by Size.
Default: off

parameters.label: Copy Layer Alpha
parameters.description:
If this is turned on, each hexagon will become partly transparent based on the alpha value of the layer at the pixel at the center of the hexagon (technically, the alpha value of the pixel is multiplied with the alpha value of Color 1, Color 2 or Color 3 respectively). If this is turned off, hexagons are rendered fully opaque (or with whatever transparency is specified by the alpha of Color 1, Color 2 or Color 3)
Default: off

parameters.label: Line Width
parameters.description:
The width of the lines between hexagons in the array, as a ratio of the hexagon size. At 0, there will be no lines between the hexagons. At 1, the lines will be just wide enough to make all of the hexagons disappear.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.1

parameters.label: Feather
parameters.description:
Softens the gaps between the hexagons.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.05

parameters.label: Angle
parameters.description:
The angle of the pattern relative to the layer.
Range: 0º to 3600º; Default: 0º

parameters.label: Screen Space
parameters.description:
If this is turned on, Offset, /Size, Stretch, and Angle become relative to the screen instead of relative to the layer.
Default: off

title: Hexagon Tile Rotate
description: Divides the layer into a grid of hexagonal tiles and rotates the pixels in each tile independently.

title: Hexagon Tile Shift
description: Divides the layer into a grid of hexagonal tiles and shifts the contents of each tile in a random direction.

title: Hexagon Tiling
description: Repeats the layer in a pattern of hexagonal tiles, filling the screen.

title: Hexagonal Prism
description: Renders a faux-3D hexagonal prism using the layer as a surface texture.

title: Highlights and Shadows
description: Independently adjusts the intensity of the lightest and darkest parts of the layer.

title: Hollow Box
description: Renders a faux-3D hollow box, using the layer as a surface texture, with sides that can be turned on or off, punched out with preset shapes, or offset outward.

parameters.label: Orientation
parameters.description:
The orientation of the 3D object. This is similar to Rotation, but is interpreted differently when animated using Keyframes. /Orientation animates along the nearest path between different poses, and is useful for posing an object or animating an object between poses. Orientation values can't go out of range, so you can't use Orientation to "spin" an object (for that, use Orientation without animation to pose the object, and animate Rotation to create the spin)
Default: 0.000000,0.000000,0.000000

parameters.label: Rotation
parameters.description:
The rotation of the 3D object. This is similar to Orientation, but the X, Y and Z components are interpreted independently when animated and can go out of range. This makes Rotation very handy for animating spinning motion, but not as convenient for creating arbitrary poses or animation between specific positions (for that, use Orientation).
Default: 0, 0, 0

parameters.label: Width
parameters.description:
The width of the object (the size along the x-axis) in proportional layer coordinates.
Range: 1 to 1000; Default: 100

parameters.label: Height
parameters.description:
The height of the object (the size along the y-axis) in proportional layer coordinates.
Range: 1 to 1000; Default: 100

parameters.label: Depth
parameters.description:
The depth of the object (the size along the z-axis, away from the viewer, into the screen)  in proportional layer coordinates.
Range: 1 to 1000; Default: 100

parameters.label: Thickness
parameters.description:
The thickness of the sides of the box in proportional layer coordinates.
Range: 1 to 1000; Default: 5

parameters.label: Outset
parameters.description:
The amount by which the sides of the box are outset from their original positions, in proportional layer coordinates. Setting this to values greater than zero pushes the sides outwards, creating gaps at the seams and "opening" the box.
Range: 0 to 1000; Default: 0

parameters.label: Smoothing
parameters.description:
Smooths the edges and corners of the shape, resulting in round points and bevels. Use zero to render the shape without any smoothing, resulting in sharp edges and corners. Higher values specify the smoothing radius in the same coordinates used to define the size and position of the shape.
Range: 0 to 500; Default: 0

parameters.label: Position
parameters.description:
The position of the shape in 3D space in proportional layer coordinates.
Default: 0, 100, 600

parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description:
A scaling factor to apply to the 3D shape after it has been projected onto the 2D layer. This makes the shape larger or smaller without affecting the perspective in 3D space.
Range: 0 to 10; Default: 1

parameters.label: Style
parameters.description:
Controls the style of the front side of the box.

Normal: The side is displayed normally.
Hidden: The side is hidden (leaving the interior of the box visible)
Circular Cutout: A circle is cut out from the center of the side (the Progress property determines the size of the circle).
Circular Crop: The side is cropped from the corners with a circular shape (the Progress property determines the size of the circle).
Square Cutout: A square is cut out from the center of the side (the Progress property determines the size of the square).
Square Crop: The side is cropped from the corners with a square shape (the Progress property determines the size of the square).

Default: Normal

parameters.label: Progress
parameters.description:
For styles such as Circular Cutout, Square Crop and so on, the progress determines how much of the cutout or crop defined by that style is applied (for Normal and Hidden sides, this is not used).
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 50%

parameters.label: Outset
parameters.description:
Controls the outset of the front side, as a percentage of the box size. Values over 50% move the side outwards; values under 50% move the side inwards. Use 50% for no outset. This outset is in addition to any outset from the overall Outset property.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 50%

parameters.description:

Normal: The side is displayed normally.
Hidden: The side is hidden (leaving the interior of the box visible)
Circular Cutout: A circle is cut out from the center of the side (the Progress property determines the size of the circle).
Circular Crop: The side is cropped from the corners with a circular shape (the Progress property determines the size of the circle).
Square Cutout: A square is cut out from the center of the side (the Progress property determines the size of the square).
Square Crop: The side is cropped from the corners with a square shape (the Progress property determines the size of the square).

Default: Normal

parameters.label: Shading
parameters.description:
Controls how the surface of the 3D shape is shaded.

Flat - Shades the shape without applying any lighting. The original colors in the layer are used as they are.
Point Light - Shades the shape as though it were lit with a point light. Point lights have a particular position in 3D space and emit light in all directions from that position. A light bulb is a point light.
Directional Light - Shades the shape as though it were lit with a directional light. Directional lights have a particular direction in 3D space, but the distance is not used. Direction lights are assumed to be infinitely distant. Directional lights are typically used for light sources that are very far away, like the sun. The difference between directional and point light sources is easiest to see in specular reflections on shiny surfaces.
Depth Map - Shades the shape in grays, where the color of each pixel is based on the distance from the viewer. Lighter pixels are closer to the viewer, while darker pixels are further into the screen. No lighting is applied to the shape.
Normal Map - Shades the shape using a normal map where the RGB values of each pixel encode the XYZ direction of a surface normal at that location on the surface of the layer. In other words, for each pixel, the values of red, green, and blue represent the X, Y, and Z values of a vector pointing outwards, perpendicular to the surface of the shape. The length of the encoded vector is always 1.
Normal & Depth Map - Shades the shape with a normal map multiplied by a depth map. In other words, the RGB values are encoded XYZ values of surface normal vectors for the shape, but the length of each vector is based on the distance from the viewer, rather than being normalized to a length of 1.

Default: Point Light

parameters.label: Intensity
parameters.description:
The brightness of the light source, used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directonal Light.
Range: 0% to 200%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Shine
parameters.description:
Controls how shiny the surface is. Higher values make the surface appear more metallic.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.8

parameters.label: Specularity
parameters.description:
The amount of specular reflectivity in the shape's surface material. Use lower values for a matte finish, and higher values for smooth, glossy surfaces.
Range: 1 to 150; Default: 30

parameters.label: Ambient
parameters.description:
The amount of ambient light in the environment. Ambient light is light that comes from all around, without a particular direction, and is useful for shapes in scenes with lots of soft, scattered light. Lower values result in less ambient light, for a harsher lighting effect and more pronounced shadows. Higher values result in less pronounced, weaker shadows.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.1

parameters.label: Color
parameters.description:
The color of the light source.
Default:

parameters.label: Direction
parameters.description:
The direction of the light source, used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directional Light.
Default: 75, -75

parameters.label: Elevation
parameters.description:
The distance of the light source from the shape (not from the screen). Positive values place the light source in front of the shape; negative values place the light source behind the shape. This is only used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directonal Light.
Range: -100 to 800; Default: 100

parameters.label: Mirror
parameters.description:
Flips alternate copies of the surface texture so that the edges meet seamlessly.
Default: off

parameters.label: Stretch
parameters.description:
Controls how textures are stretched when mapped to the surface of the shape.
When turned off, textures are mapped based on the size of the shape. For example, if the shape has a height of 300, the texture will be tiled 3 times vertically along the height of the shape. This is useful for textures that should repeat more as the shape gets larger (for example, stucco, wood grain, sand, and so on).
When turned on, textures are mapped so that one repetition of the texture exactly matches the size of the shape, which is useful if you want the texture to stretch or compress as the shape's size changes.
Note: The stretch setting is applied before Repeat X and Repeat Y, so for example, if stretch is turned on and Repeat Y is set to 2, there will always be 2 copies of the texture vertically along the hight of the shape, and those copies will stretch to match the height of the shape if it changes. Similarly if stretch is turned off and Repeat Y is set to 2, there will be two repetitions of the texture for each unit of height.
Default: off

parameters.label: Repeat X
parameters.description:
Number of times to repeat the layer texture horizontally.
Range: 0.01 to 20; Default: 1

parameters.label: Repeat Y
parameters.description:
Number of times to repeat the layer texture vertically.
Range: 0.01 to 20; Default: 1

parameters.label: Angle
parameters.description:
Rotates the surface texture of the shape (to rotate the individual texture tiles, use Twirl instead.)
Range: -3600º to 3600º; Default: 0º

parameters.label: Crop
parameters.description:
Controls cropping of the layer before it is used as a texture.
Values less than zero result in cropping of the layer, and values greater than zero add extra transparent space around the edges of the layer. Set this to zero for no cropping.
This is typically used to remove the transparent fringe or gap around layers that don't perfectly fill their bounding box, so that the layer tiles properly when used as a texture.
Range: 0.01 to 2; Default: 1

parameters.label: Twirl
parameters.description:
Rotates texture instances before tiling (which is different to Angle, which rotates the surface texture as a whole).
Range: -3600º to 3600º; Default: 0º

parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description:
Shifts the surface texture by the given amount. Use this to adjust the alignment of the texture on the surface of the object, or animate it for a moving surface.
Default: 0, 0

parameters.label: Precision
parameters.description:
Controls how precisely the 3D object is rendered by adjusting the number of iterations used when ray marching. More iterations will yield higher-quality results, especially for the edges of objects. Fewer iterations will give faster preview and export performance. For best results, a setting between 30 and 60 is recommended.
Range: 10 to 200; Default: 30

title: Hot Color
description: Remaps a grayscale image (or luminance values of a color image) into an intense color ramp based on two selected hues.

title: Hue Shift
description: Rotates the color hues in the layer.

title: Inner Blur
description: Blurs the opaque parts of the layer without blurring the opacity.

title: Inner Glow
description: Draws a glow along the inside edges of the opaque parts the layer.

title: Inner Pinch/Bulge
description: Distorts the layer by pinching it towards the center or expanding it outwards, without affecting the edges or opacity of the layer.

title: Invert
description: Inverts the layer's red, green, or blue channels independently.

title: Iridescence
description: Renders a flowing iridescent pattern in opaque parts of the layer.

title: Kaleidoscope
description: Creates symmetric radial reflections of the layer.

title: Lens Blur
description: Applies an angular blur to the layer, excluding the central area.

title: Lens Flare
description: Renders a lens flare in the layer.

title: Light Glow
description: Creates a soft glow and optional halo around the lighter areas within a photo or video layer based on a luminance threshold.

parameters.label: Diffusion
parameters.description:
Controls the softness of the glow as it spreads outwards from the light parts of the layer.
Range: 0 to 2; Default: 0.25

parameters.label: Threshold
parameters.description:
A luminance threshold that controls which pixels emit the light glow. Pixels with luminance over this threshold will have a light glow; pixels with luminance under this threshold will not glow at all.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.7

parameters.label: Intensity
parameters.description:
Controls the brightness of the glow. At the default of 1.0, light areas will not get brighter, but will emit a glow into nearby darker areas. For higher values of intensity, the light areas of the layer will become even lighter and will emit an even stronger glow into nearby darker areas.
Range: 0 to 5; Default: 1

parameters.label: Color
parameters.description:
A color to use instead of the original pixel color when rendering the glow.
Default:

parameters.label: Blend
parameters.description:
Controls the color of the glow. At 0% the original pixel color is used; at 100% the Color property is used. Values in between mix the original pixel color and glow color proportionally. The final mixed color is blended on top of the layer using the Screen blending mode.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 25%

parameters.label: Halo
parameters.description:
Creates a glowing ring in addition to the basic glow, similar to the first ring of the Airy pattern that occurs around an area of bloom. The value of Halo determines the brightness of the ring relative to the setting given for Intensity.
Range: 0 to 5; Default: 0

parameters.label: Alpha
parameters.description:
Controls the opacity of the glow and the halo.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 75%

title: Lightning
description: Renders fractal arcs of lightning between two points in the layer.

title: Linear Repeat
description: Dynamically generates multiple copies of the layer, arranged along a line.

title: Linear Streaks
description: Creates line-shaped streaks by sliding the layer at an angle and blending together overlapping pixels.

title: Long Shadow
description: Renders long parallel shadows extending from opaque parts of the layer.

title: Luma Key
description: Keys out all pixels lighter than or darker than a given threshold, making those parts of the layer transparent.

title: Magnify Background
description: Makes the layer transparent and magnifies the area behind the layer, as if the layer were a magnifying glass.

parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description:
The amount by which to magnify the area behind the layer.
Range: 0.01 to 50; Default: 1

parameters.label: Magnify Layer
parameters.description:
Controls whether the layer itself is also magnified. When this is turned on, the layer and the background are both magnified. When this is turned off, the layer bounds remain the same, and only the background viewed through the layer is magnified (much like a magnifying class).
Default: off

parameters.label: Sampling
parameters.description:
The sampling mode used when re-sampling the background as it is magnified.

Nearest: The nearest pixel color is used. This results in hard edges between pixels (each pixel will look like a little square). This is useful when using the effect to examine individual pixels.
Linear: The nearest four pixel colors are blended linearly, softening the edges between the enlarged pixels. Use this if you want the magnified area to look less blocky.

Default: Linear

title: Mask Blur
description: Blurs the background behind the layer, with the blur strength based on the layer luminance.

title: Matte Choker
description: Expands the transparent parts of the layer, shrinking the opaque parts.

title: Mirror
description: Overlays the layer with a copy of itself mirrored along either the horizontal or vertical axis, with optional alpha and blending mode control.

title: Mosaic
description: Divides the layer into array of squares and averages the color of each square, resulting in a pixelated look.

title: Motion Blur
description: Simulates motion blur by blurring the layer based on position, rotation, and scale keyframe animation from the layer's Move & Transform settings, as well as motion from certain other effects that are compatible with motion blur.

parameters.label: Tune
parameters.description:
Adjusts the amount of motion blur applied.
Range: 0 to 4; Default: 1

parameters.label: Position
parameters.description:
Controls whether changes in position will cause motion blur.
Default: on

parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description:
Controls whether changes in scale will cause motion blur.
Default: on

parameters.label: Angle
parameters.description:
Controls whether changes in angle (rotation) will cause motion blur.
Default: on

title: Move Along Path
description: Positions the layer at a point partway way along the outline path of the layer immediately beneath.

title: Noise
description: Blends random noise with the opaque parts of the layer.

title: Octahedron
description: Renders a faux-3D regular octahedron using the layer as a surface texture.

title: Offset
description: Offsets the layer content, wrapping at the edges of the bounding box of the layer.

title: Omino Diffusion+
description: Renders a photo or video layer with a limited palette using error diffusion, but damping or exaggerating the quantization errors to create intentional distortions. Individual images become unrecognizable, but action can be seen in the moving aggregate. This effect is based on the Omino Suite, with special thanks to David Van Brink.

parameters.label: Angle
parameters.description:
The angle along which quantization errors are diffused. At an angle of 0º, errors are diffused from left to right, with angles increasing clockwise.
Range: 0º to 3600º; Default: 0º

parameters.label: Strength
parameters.description:
The distance over which quantization errors are diffused, as a ratio of the size of the layer.
Range: 0.01 to 1; Default: 1

parameters.label: Steps
parameters.description:
The number of samples along the direction specified by Angle which contribute to the final pixel color.
Range: 1 to 200; Default: 35

parameters.label: Error Weight
parameters.description:
A weighting factor applied to the residual error remaining after each pixel color is quantized. If this is set to 1.0, this effect behaves similarly to regular error diffusion dithering (except that, unlike Floyd–Steinberg dithering, residual quantization errors are not spread into other rows of pixels). The interesting patterns and unusual characteristics of the Omino Diffusion+ effect are achieved by intentionally amplifying errors by setting the weight to higher values. Setting the weight to zero will yield results similar to the Palette Map effect.
Range: 0 to 10; Default: 0.75

parameters.label: Weight Falloff
parameters.description:
An exponential decay factor applied to Error Weight used for more distant samples (the decay causes the error weight for more distant samples to approach 1.0)
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 0%

parameters.label: Palette
parameters.description:
The color palette used to quantize the colors in the image. A variety of preset palettes are available, or you can select one of the custom palette options to choose colors manually.

Black/White
RGB Posterize, 2 Levels
RGB Posterize, 3 Levels
RGB Posterize, 4 Levels
Gray, 3 Levels
Gray, 4 Levels
CGA Green/Red/Yellow
CGA Cyan/Magenta/White
CGA Blue/Cyan/Magenta/White
Saturate and Posterize
Custom 2-Color
Custom 3-Color
Custom 4-Color
Custom 5-Color
Custom 6-Color

Default: RGB Posterize

parameters.label: Alpha
parameters.description:
Controls the opacity of the effect. When set to 1.0 the effect output completely overwrites the original colors in the layer; reduce this to make the effect partly transparent, blending the effect output with the original layer colors.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 1

parameters.label: Blending Mode
parameters.description:
Controls how the effect is blended with the original colors in the layer.

Normal: The effect completely overwrites the original layer colors.
Multiply: The standard Multiply blending mode: The output of the effect is multiplied with the original color of each pixel in the layer (this results in darker colors being used).
Screen: The standard Screen blending mode: The inverted output of the effect is multiplied with the inverted original color of each pixel in the layer, and the result is then inverted one last time (this results in lighter colors being used).

Default: Normal

parameters.label: Color 1
parameters.description:
These colors define the custom palette used when Palette is set to one of the Custom N-color options.
Default:

parameters.label: Color 2
parameters.description:
These colors define the custom palette used when Palette is set to one of the Custom N-color options.
Default:

parameters.label: Color 3
parameters.description:
These colors define the custom palette used when Palette is set to one of the Custom N-color options.
Default:

parameters.label: Color 4
parameters.description:
These colors define the custom palette used when Palette is set to one of the Custom N-color options.
Default:

parameters.label: Color 5
parameters.description:
These colors define the custom palette used when Palette is set to one of the Custom N-color options.
Default:

parameters.label: Color 6
parameters.description:
These colors define the custom palette used when Palette is set to one of the Custom N-color options.
Default:

title: Omino Glass
description: Renders the layer as though it were made of glass, with refraction and a glossy contour, and chromatic aberration based on segmentation of the visible spectrum into seven regions. The layer luminance may optionally be used as a height map. This effect is based on the Omino Suite, with special thanks to David Van Brink.

parameters.label: Bevel
parameters.description:
Determines the amount of smooth beveling added around the edges of the opaque parts of the layer.
Range: 0 to 2; Default: 0.1

parameters.label: Refraction
parameters.description:
The amount of refraction applied where there are changes in the height map or bevel curvature. This is a maximum refraction distance in pixels.
Range: -100 to 100; Default: 5

parameters.label: Chromatic Aberration
parameters.description:
The amount of chromatic aberration applied where there are changes in the height map or bevel curvature. This is similar to the Refraction setting but only operates on pixel hues.
Range: -100 to 100; Default: 5

parameters.label: Feature Size
parameters.description:
The overall amount that surface features (changes in the height map or bevel curvature) affect refraction and chromatic aberration (if this is reduced to zero, Refraction and Chromatic Aberration are ignored)
Range: 5 to 500; Default: 100

parameters.label: Smoothing Samples
parameters.description:
The number of height map samples that are used to calculate the surface normals used for chromatic aberration and refraction. If this is too low, banding can occur because the change from one pixel to the next causes aliasing in the surface normals. As this increases, more nearby pixels are averaged to compute the surface normal, so less aliasing happens, but details in the surface will also become more smoothed out.
Range: 1 to 20; Default: 2

parameters.label: Color
parameters.description:
A tint color that is combined with whatever is behind the layer, based on the setting of the Blend parameter. This color is combined using Multiply blending so it simulates the look of colored glass.
Default:

parameters.label: Blend
parameters.description:
The amount of Color applied to tint the glass.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 0%

parameters.label: Shading
parameters.description:
Controls how the surface of the shape defined by the bevel, roughness, and height map is shaded.

None: No lighting is applied (only refraction and chromatic aberration).
Point Light: Shades the shape as though it were lit with a point light. Point lights have a particular position in 3D space and emit light in all directions from that position. A light bulb is a point light.
Directional Light: Shades the shape as though it were lit with a directional light. Directional lights have a particular direction in 3D space, but the distance is not used. Direction lights are assumed to be infinitely distant. Directional lights are typically used for light sources that are very far away, like the sun. The difference between directional and point light sources is easiest to see in specular reflections on shiny surfaces.

Default: Point Light

parameters.label: Intensity
parameters.description:
The brightness of the light source, used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directonal Light.
Range: 0% to 200%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Shine
parameters.description:
Controls how shiny the surface is. Higher values make the surface appear more metallic.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.8

parameters.label: Specularity
parameters.description:
The amount of specular reflectivity in the shape's surface material. Use lower values for a matte finish, and higher values for smooth, glossy surfaces.
Range: 1 to 250; Default: 30

parameters.label: Ambient
parameters.description:
The amount of ambient light in the environment. Ambient light is light that comes from all around, without a particular direction, and is useful for shapes in scenes with lots of soft, scattered light. Lower values result in less ambient light, for a harsher lighting effect and more pronounced shadows. Higher values result in less pronounced, weaker shadows.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.1

parameters.label: Color
parameters.description:
The color of the light source.
Default:

parameters.label: Direction
parameters.description:
The direction of the light source, used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directional Light.
Default: 75, -75

parameters.label: Elevation
parameters.description:
The distance of the light source from the shape (not from the screen). Positive values place the light source in front of the shape; negative values place the light source behind the shape. This is only used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directonal Light.
Range: -100 to 800; Default: 100

parameters.label: Use Layer Luminance
parameters.description:
When turned on, the luminance of pixels in the layer is used as a height map, affecting lighting, chromatic aberration, and refraction.
Default: on

parameters.label: Amount
parameters.description:
The amount of randomly generated roughness to apply to the height map (in addition to any roughness from the layer luminance or curvature from the bevel).
Range: -100% to 100%; Default: 0%

parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description:
Scales the randomly generated roughness.
Range: 0.01 to 50; Default: 1

parameters.label: Evolution
parameters.description:
Controls small, gradual changes in the random pattern of roughness in the layer surface when Amount is greater than zero. Animate this to produce flowing motion.
Range: -50 to 50; Default: 0

parameters.label: Seed
parameters.description:
A value used to generate the pattern of random roughness in the layer surface when Amount is greater than zero. Change this for a different pattern of roughness. Use keyframe animation to make the roughness change radically every frame.
Range: 0 to 5; Default: 0

title: Oscillate
description: Moves the layer back and forth repeatedly.

parameters.label: Angle
parameters.description:
The direction along which the layer will move back and forth, measured clockwise with 0º representing horizontal motion (right first, then left) and 90º representing vertical motion (down first, then up). To move in the opposite direction first, add 180º.
Range: -3600º to 3600º; Default: 45º

parameters.label: Frequency
parameters.description:
How fast the layer will back and forth, in hertz (cycles per second). At a frequency of 1.0, the layer will move back and forth, returning to its original position after exactly a second. This is calculated cumulatively, so you can use keyframe animation to make the oscillation speed up and slow down naturally.
Range: 0 to 16; Default: 2

parameters.label: Magnitude
parameters.description:
The furthest distance the layer moves from the original position, in pixels.
Range: 0 to 4000; Default: 25

parameters.label: Wave
parameters.description:
Controls the type of motion used to oscillate the layer.

Sine - Oscillation will based on a sine wave, resulting in smoother motion that slows down before reversing direction.
Triangle - Oscillation will be based on a triangle wave, resulting in motion at a constant speed. Reversal of direction will feel abrupt.

Default: Sine

parameters.label: Phase
parameters.description:
Adds an offset into the oscillation cycle. This can be used to start the oscillation at a different point in the cycle (rather than at the original position of the layer) or to manually control the progress of the animation (by animating Phase) if Frequency is zero.
Range: 0 to 1000; Default: 0

title: Palette Map
description: Maps pixel colors in the layer to the closest matching color in a palette.

title: Pinch/Bulge
description: Distorts the layer by pinching it towards the center or expanding it outward.

title: Pixelate
description: Decreases the layer resolution so that individual pixels become visible, sampling at the centers of the pixels, and drawing each pixel with an optional vignette.

title: Polar Coordinates
description: Warps the layer by mapping from cartesian coordinates to polar coordinates.

title: Polar Displacement Map
description: Angularly displaces pixels in the background based on the color of pixels in the layer.

title: Posterize
description: Decreases the number of step in the layer's color channels.

title: Precise Box Blur
description: Performs a slow but precise box blur of the layer.

title: Pulse Opacity
description: Automatically pulses the layer opacity over time, based on a sine wave or triangular wave.

title: Pulse Size
description: Automatically grows and shrinks the layer repeatedly over time, based on a sine wave or triangular wave.

title: Pyramid
description: Renders a faux-3D regular pyramid with a square base, using the layer as a surface texture.

title: RGB Split
description: Splits the red, green and blue channels of the layer, keeping one fixed in position and shifting the other two channels in opposite directions.

title: Radial Rays
description: Overlays multiple solid-color radial rays in the opaque parts of the layer.

title: Radial Repeat
description: Dynamically generates multiple copies of the layer, arranged in a circular pattern.

title: Radial Shadow
description: Renders long shadows radiating away from a point, based on the opaque parts of the layer.

title: Radial Wipe
description: Masks out a region of the layer bounded by two radial lines.

title: Random Displacement
description: Automatically moves the layer smoothly but randomly over time

title: Random Jitter
description: Automatically jumps the layer randomly back and forth over time, along a selected angle.

title: Raster Extrude
description: Renders a faux-3D raster extrusion of the layer based on the opaque and transparent pixels within the layer, using the opaque parts of the layer as a surface texture for the front and back of the extrusion.

parameters.label: Orientation
parameters.description:
The orientation of the 3D object. This is similar to Rotation, but is interpreted differently when animated using Keyframes. /Orientation animates along the nearest path between different poses, and is useful for posing an object or animating an object between poses. Orientation values can't go out of range, so you can't use Orientation to "spin" an object (for that, use Orientation without animation to pose the object, and animate Rotation to create the spin)
Default: 0.000000,-0.000000,0.000000

parameters.label: Rotation
parameters.description:
The rotation of the 3D object. This is similar to Orientation, but the X, Y and Z components are interpreted independently when animated and can go out of range. This makes Rotation very handy for animating spinning motion, but not as convenient for creating arbitrary poses or animation between specific positions (for that, use Orientation).
Default: 0, 0, 0

parameters.label: Width
parameters.description:
The width of the object (the size along the x-axis) in proportional layer coordinates.
Range: 1% to 1000%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Height
parameters.description:
The height of the object (the size along the y-axis) in proportional layer coordinates.
Range: 1% to 1000%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Depth
parameters.description:
The depth of the object (the size along the z-axis, away from the viewer, into the screen)  in proportional layer coordinates.
Range: 1 to 1000; Default: 10

parameters.label: Position
parameters.description:
The position of the shape in 3D space in proportional layer coordinates.
Default: 0, 100, 500

parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description:
A scaling factor to apply to the 3D shape after it has been projected onto the 2D layer. This makes the shape larger or smaller without affecting the perspective in 3D space.
Range: 0 to 10; Default: 1

parameters.label: Interior Color
parameters.description:
The color to use for the interior of the object, revealed by extrusion. Note that for the to be effective, you also must adjust Interior.
Default:

parameters.label: Interior
parameters.description:
Controls how much of the interior color is used for the extruded part of the 3D object. When this is 0% the interior color is not used, and the color of the pixels near the edges of the original layer are used. When this is 100%, the edge pixels are ignored and only Interior Color is applied.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 0%

parameters.label: Fade Color
parameters.description:
This is similar to Interior Color but is applied in a gradient that is stronger toward the back part of the extruded volume. Use this if you want the extruded sides of the shape to fade towards a different color in the back.  Note that for this to be effective, you also must adjust Fade.
Default:

parameters.label: Fade
parameters.description:
Controls how much of the Fade Color is applied to the extruded volume. When this is 100%, the back part of the extruded volume fully uses the fade color. For smaller values, the fade color becomes transparent, and at 0% it is ignored.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 0%

parameters.label: Shading
parameters.description:
Controls how the surface of the 3D shape is shaded.

Flat - Shades the shape without applying any lighting. The original colors in the layer are used as they are.
Point Light - Shades the shape as though it were lit with a point light. Point lights have a particular position in 3D space and emit light in all directions from that position. A light bulb is a point light.
Directional Light - Shades the shape as though it were lit with a directional light. Directional lights have a particular direction in 3D space, but the distance is not used. Direction lights are assumed to be infinitely distant. Directional lights are typically used for light sources that are very far away, like the sun. The difference between directional and point light sources is easiest to see in specular reflections on shiny surfaces.
Depth Map - Shades the shape in grays, where the color of each pixel is based on the distance from the viewer. Lighter pixels are closer to the viewer, while darker pixels are further into the screen. No lighting is applied to the shape.
Normal Map - Shades the shape using a normal map where the RGB values of each pixel encode the XYZ direction of a surface normal at that location on the surface of the layer. In other words, for each pixel, the values of red, green, and blue represent the X, Y, and Z values of a vector pointing outwards, perpendicular to the surface of the shape. The length of the encoded vector is always 1.
Normal & Depth Map - Shades the shape with a normal map multiplied by a depth map. In other words, the RGB values are encoded XYZ values of surface normal vectors for the shape, but the length of each vector is based on the distance from the viewer, rather than being normalized to a length of 1.

Default: Point Light

parameters.label: Intensity
parameters.description:
The brightness of the light source, used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directonal Light.
Range: 0% to 200%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Shine
parameters.description:
Controls how shiny the surface is. Higher values make the surface appear more metallic.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.8

parameters.label: Specularity
parameters.description:
The amount of specular reflectivity in the shape's surface material. Use lower values for a matte finish, and higher values for smooth, glossy surfaces.
Range: 1 to 150; Default: 30

parameters.label: Ambient
parameters.description:
The amount of ambient light in the environment. Ambient light is light that comes from all around, without a particular direction, and is useful for shapes in scenes with lots of soft, scattered light. Lower values result in less ambient light, for a harsher lighting effect and more pronounced shadows. Higher values result in less pronounced, weaker shadows.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.1

parameters.label: Color
parameters.description:
The color of the light source.
Default:

parameters.label: Direction
parameters.description:
The direction of the light source, used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directional Light.
Default: 75, -75

parameters.label: Elevation
parameters.description:
The distance of the light source from the shape (not from the screen). Positive values place the light source in front of the shape; negative values place the light source behind the shape. This is only used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directonal Light.
Range: -100 to 800; Default: 100

parameters.label: Precision
parameters.description:
Controls how precisely the 3D object is rendered by adjusting the number of iterations used when ray marching. More iterations will yield higher-quality results, especially for the edges of objects. Fewer iterations will give faster preview and export performance. For best results, a setting between 30 and 60 is recommended.
Range: 10 to 800; Default: 200

title: Rays
description: Renders light rays from bright parts of the layer, based on the threshold setting.

title: Repeat
description: Dynamically generates multiple copies of the layer.

title: Repeat Along Path
description: Dynamically generates multiple copies of the layer along the outline path of the layer immediately beneath.

title: Replace Color
description: Replaces one color in the layer with another.

title: Ribbon
description: Renders a twisted, flowing ribbon of varying width in the opaque parts of the layer.

title: Roughen Edges
description: Roughens the edges of the opaque parts of the layer.

title: Saturation / Vibrance
description: Adjusts the saturation or purity of colors in the layer.

title: Scatter Repeat
description: Dynamically generates multiple copies of the layer, scattered randomly around the original layer location.

title: Sharpen
description: Enhances details in the layer by increasing the contrast wherever there are abrupt differences in the luminance of nearby pixels in the layer.

title: Simple Starfield
description: Renders a simple parallax starfield pattern, with a depth component, in the opaque parts of the layer.

title: Smooth Bevel
description: Draws a smooth curved bevel inside the edges of the opaque parts of the layer.

title: Smooth Edges
description: Smooths the edges of the opaque parts of a layer without affecting the pixel colors within the layer.

parameters.label: Strength
parameters.description:
The amount of smoothing to apply. Set to zero for no smoothing (the original layer will be unchanged).
Range: 0 to 2; Default: 0.02

parameters.label: Mask To Layer
parameters.description:
Turn this on to mask the output of the effect to the original layer's alpha.
Default: off

title: Soft Glow
description: Creates a soft Gaussian-based glow within a photo or video layer, or around the outside edge of any layer.

parameters.label: Diffusion
parameters.description:
Controls the softness of the glow.
Range: 0 to 2; Default: 0.1

parameters.label: Brightness
parameters.description:
The brightness of the glow. At 100%, light areas will not get brighter, but will emit a glow into nearby darker areas. At 0% the glow will disappear. At values greater than 100%, the entire image will become brighter, with lighter areas becoming particularly brighter.
Range: 0% to 200%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Contrast
parameters.description:
Controls the contrast between light and dark parts of the blurred image prior to blending, essentially squeezing the glow out of darker parts of the image.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 20%

parameters.label: Highlights
parameters.description:
Adds white to the brighter parts of the glow, rather than using the original color or blend color for those parts.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 50%

parameters.label: Color
parameters.description:
A color to use instead of the original pixel color when rendering the glow.
Default:

parameters.label: Blend
parameters.description:
Controls the color of the glow. At 0% the original pixel color is used; at 100% the Color property is used. Values in between mix the original pixel color and glow color proportionally. The final mixed color is blended on top of the layer using the Screen blending mode.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 25%

parameters.label: Alpha
parameters.description:
Controls the opacity of the glow inside the layer.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Outer Glow
parameters.description:
Controls the opacity of the glow outside the layer.
Range: 0% to 100%; Default: 0%

title: Solid Color
description: Fills the opaque parts of the layer with a solid color.

title: Solid Matte
description: Composites the layer on a solid color matte, filling transparent parts of the layer with a solid background color.

title: Spectral Map
description: Maps each color in the layer into a custom spectrum of colors based on the brightness of the original color.

title: Spherize
description: Renders a faux-3D sphere, interpreting the layer as an equirectangular projection and using it as a surface texture for the sphere.

title: Spin
description: Automatically spins the layer at a given speed.

title: Spin Blur
description: Applies an angular blur to the layer, as though it were spinning.

title: Spin Streaks
description: Creates arc-shaped streaks by spinning the layer around a point and blending together overlapping pixels.

title: Spot Color
description: Keeps one color while decreasing the saturation of other colors in the layer.

title: Squeeze
description: Distorts the layer by squeezing it along one axis while expanding it along the other axis.

title: Star
description: Renders an individual star in the current layer.

title: Star Polyhedron
description: Renders a faux-3D star polyhedron using the layer as a surface texture.

parameters.label: Orientation
parameters.description:
The orientation of the 3D object. This is similar to Rotation, but is interpreted differently when animated using Keyframes. /Orientation animates along the nearest path between different poses, and is useful for posing an object or animating an object between poses. Orientation values can't go out of range, so you can't use Orientation to "spin" an object (for that, use Orientation without animation to pose the object, and animate Rotation to create the spin)
Default: 0.000000,-0.000000,0.000000

parameters.label: Rotation
parameters.description:
The rotation of the 3D object. This is similar to Orientation, but the X, Y and Z components are interpreted independently when animated and can go out of range. This makes Rotation very handy for animating spinning motion, but not as convenient for creating arbitrary poses or animation between specific positions (for that, use Orientation).
Default: 0, 0, 0

parameters.label: Size
parameters.description:
The overall size of the shape.
Range: 1 to 1000; Default: 50

parameters.label: Depth
parameters.description:
The depth or thickness of the start from the front center where the arms meet to the back center.
Range: 15 to 1000; Default: 50

parameters.label: Point Length
parameters.description:
The length of the points of the star. This is distinct from Size, as this only affects the points and not the central portion where the arms of the star meet.
Range: 100 to 1000; Default: 100

parameters.label: Smoothing
parameters.description:
Smooths the edges and corners of the shape, resulting in round points and bevels. Use zero to render the shape without any smoothing, resulting in sharp edges and corners. Higher values specify the smoothing radius in the same coordinates used to define the size and position of the shape.
Range: 0 to 5; Default: 0

parameters.label: Position
parameters.description:
The position of the shape in 3D space in proportional layer coordinates.
Default: 0, 100, 500

parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description:
A scaling factor to apply to the 3D shape after it has been projected onto the 2D layer. This makes the shape larger or smaller without affecting the perspective in 3D space.
Range: 0 to 10; Default: 3

parameters.label: Points
parameters.description:
The number of points on the star.
Range: 2 to 16; Default: 5

parameters.label: Shading
parameters.description:
Controls how the surface of the 3D shape is shaded.

Flat - Shades the shape without applying any lighting. The original colors in the layer are used as they are.
Point Light - Shades the shape as though it were lit with a point light. Point lights have a particular position in 3D space and emit light in all directions from that position. A light bulb is a point light.
Directional Light - Shades the shape as though it were lit with a directional light. Directional lights have a particular direction in 3D space, but the distance is not used. Direction lights are assumed to be infinitely distant. Directional lights are typically used for light sources that are very far away, like the sun. The difference between directional and point light sources is easiest to see in specular reflections on shiny surfaces.
Depth Map - Shades the shape in grays, where the color of each pixel is based on the distance from the viewer. Lighter pixels are closer to the viewer, while darker pixels are further into the screen. No lighting is applied to the shape.
Normal Map - Shades the shape using a normal map where the RGB values of each pixel encode the XYZ direction of a surface normal at that location on the surface of the layer. In other words, for each pixel, the values of red, green, and blue represent the X, Y, and Z values of a vector pointing outwards, perpendicular to the surface of the shape. The length of the encoded vector is always 1.
Normal & Depth Map - Shades the shape with a normal map multiplied by a depth map. In other words, the RGB values are encoded XYZ values of surface normal vectors for the shape, but the length of each vector is based on the distance from the viewer, rather than being normalized to a length of 1.

Default: Point Light

parameters.label: Intensity
parameters.description:
The brightness of the light source, used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directonal Light.
Range: 0% to 200%; Default: 100%

parameters.label: Shine
parameters.description:
Controls how shiny the surface is. Higher values make the surface appear more metallic.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.8

parameters.label: Specularity
parameters.description:
The amount of specular reflectivity in the shape's surface material. Use lower values for a matte finish, and higher values for smooth, glossy surfaces.
Range: 1 to 150; Default: 30

parameters.label: Ambient
parameters.description:
The amount of ambient light in the environment. Ambient light is light that comes from all around, without a particular direction, and is useful for shapes in scenes with lots of soft, scattered light. Lower values result in less ambient light, for a harsher lighting effect and more pronounced shadows. Higher values result in less pronounced, weaker shadows.
Range: 0 to 1; Default: 0.1

parameters.label: Color
parameters.description:
The color of the light source.
Default:

parameters.label: Direction
parameters.description:
The direction of the light source, used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directional Light.
Default: 75, -75

parameters.label: Elevation
parameters.description:
The distance of the light source from the shape (not from the screen). Positive values place the light source in front of the shape; negative values place the light source behind the shape. This is only used when Shading is set to Point Light or Directonal Light.
Range: -100 to 800; Default: 100

parameters.label: Mirror
parameters.description:
Flips alternate copies of the surface texture so that the edges meet seamlessly.
Default: off

parameters.label: Repeat X
parameters.description:
Number of times to repeat the layer texture horizontally.
Range: 0.01 to 20; Default: 1

parameters.label: Repeat Y
parameters.description:
Number of times to repeat the layer texture vertically.
Range: 0.01 to 20; Default: 1

parameters.label: Angle
parameters.description:
Rotates the surface texture of the shape (to rotate the individual texture tiles, use Twirl instead.)
Range: -3600º to 3600º; Default: 0º

parameters.label: Crop
parameters.description:
Controls cropping of the layer before it is used as a texture.
Values less than zero result in cropping of the layer, and values greater than zero add extra transparent space around the edges of the layer. Set this to zero for no cropping.
This is typically used to remove the transparent fringe or gap around layers that don't perfectly fill their bounding box, so that the layer tiles properly when used as a texture.
Range: 0.01 to 2; Default: 1

parameters.label: Twirl
parameters.description:
Rotates texture instances before tiling (which is different to Angle, which rotates the surface texture as a whole).
Range: -3600º to 3600º; Default: 0º

parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description:
Shifts the surface texture by the given amount. Use this to adjust the alignment of the texture on the surface of the object, or animate it for a moving surface.
Default: 0, 0

parameters.label: Precision
parameters.description:
Controls how precisely the 3D object is rendered by adjusting the number of iterations used when ray marching. More iterations will yield higher-quality results, especially for the edges of objects. Fewer iterations will give faster preview and export performance. For best results, a setting between 30 and 60 is recommended.
Range: 10 to 200; Default: 60

title: Star Prism
description: Renders a faux-3D star prism using the layer as a surface texture.

title: Starfield
description: Renders a complex parallax starfield that can be animated in two dimensions.

title: Stretch Axis
description: Stretches the layer in an arbitrary direction.

title: Stretch Segment
description: Slices the layer along an angle and then moves the pieces apart, stretching the pixels between them.

title: Stripes
description: Renders a pattern of stripes in the opaque parts of the layer.

title: Stroke Color
description: Overrides the layer's stroke color.

title: Stroke Taper
description: Tapers the strokes of a freehand drawing layer.

title: Swing
description: Automatically rotates the layer back and forth over time.

title: Swirl
description: Warps the layer by rotating it different amounts based on the distance from a center-point, resulting in a vortex or whirlpool-like distortion.

title: Text Progress
description: Partially displays a text layer, character by character.

title: Text Randomizer
description: Randomly changes individual characters in a text layer.

title: Text Spacing
description: Adjusts the spacing between individual letters or between lines of text in a text layer.

title: Text Transform
description: Transforms individual letters, words, lines, or pieces of letters within a specified range of a text layer.

title: Three-axis Cross
description: Renders a faux-3D three-axis cross using the layer as a surface texture.

title: Threshold
description: Converts the layer to black and white with a hard cutoff at the specified luminance threshold.

title: Tile Rotate
description: Divides the layer into a grid of tiles and rotates the pixels in each tile independently.

title: Tile Shift
description: Divides the layer into a grid of tiles and shifts the contents of each tile in a random direction.

title: Tiles
description: Repeats the layer in a tile pattern, filling the screen.

parameters.label: Crop
parameters.description:
Crops the layer before tiling it. Set this to zero for no cropping. Positive values crop normally, and negative values add a transparent edge around each tile.
Range: 0 to 20; Default: 1

parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description:
Shifts alternate rows of tiles. By default, rows are shifted horizontally, but Offset Vertically can be turned on to shift alternate columns vertically instead. Each whole-number increments shifts by a whole tile, so setting this to 0.5 will shift by exactly half a tile.
Range: -100 to 100; Default: 0

parameters.label: Mirror
parameters.description:
Turn on to mirror alternate tiles (every other column is flipped horizontally, and every other row is flipped vertically).
Default: off

parameters.label: Offset Vertically
parameters.description:
When turned on, changes the behavior of Offset so that alternate columns are offset vertically. When off, Offset works normally, offsetting alternate rows horizontally.
Default: off

parameters.label: Angle
parameters.description:
Rotates each tile independently of the others (to rotate the entire grid of tiles together, use the rotation property in Move & Transform)
Range: -1800º to 1800º; Default: 0º

title: Time Quantization
description: Causes time to pass in steps, but just for this layer.

title: Timecode
description: Replaces the text in the layer with a running timecode.

title: Torus
description: Renders a faux-3D torus using the layer as a surface texture.

title: Tunnel
description: Renders a round faux-3D tunnel using the layer as the interior surface texture.

title: Turbulence
description: Renders smooth patterns of procedurally-generated fractal noise in the opaque parts of the layer, useful for simulating real-world phenomena like clouds, or for use as a luminance map.

title: Turbulent Displace
description: Distorts the layer along selected coordinate axes using a random pattern of smooth fractal noise. Distortion may be along one or more axis in either cartesian or polar coordinate space. Useful for simulating distortions caused by real-world phenomena such as fire, water, and heat, or for modeling real-world objects that are naturally irregular such as mountains.

title: Unsharp Mask
description: Enhances the perceptual sharpness or "acutance" of a photo or video by increasing local contrast near edges, while avoiding changes to areas that were originally smooth.

parameters.label: Strength
parameters.description:
The size of the edges that are sharpened by the effect. Lower strength cause smaller scale details to be enhanced. Larger strength focuses on larger scale details, but can result in visual gradients appearing near hard edges. Essentially, this determines how close the "local" is in "local contrast".
Range: 0 to 2; Default: 0.02

parameters.label: Amount
parameters.description:
Controls the amount of local contrast added.
Range: 0% to 200%; Default: 50%

parameters.label: Threshold
parameters.description:
Controls the minimum amount of brightness change needed before something is detected as an edge. This is used to exclude smooth areas so they are not sharpened.
Range: -50% to 100%; Default: 50%

parameters.label: Show Mask
parameters.description:
Enable this to visualize the mask that is used for sharpening. This can be helpful when making fine adjustments to Strength and Threshold.
Default: off

title: Vignette
description: Darkens the edges of the layer.

title: Voronoi Cells
description: Renders procedurally-generated pattern of Voronoi cells based on randomly placed points.

title: Wave Warp
description: Distorts the layer based on a sine wave.

title: Wipe
description: Masks out rectangular segments from opposite sides of the layer. Use keyframe animation to create a wipe transition.

title: Zoom Blur
description: Applies a radial blur to the layer, as though zooming in or out.

title: Zoom Streaks
description: Creates radial streaks by scaling the layer around a point and blending together overlapping pixels.
